post_rendered:
Welcome to Mafia Game 209
Newbie Game 84
Less Crowded Mafia

Welcome to Where it’s Less Crowded: a place with a name that, if you remove the capital letters, is as innocent-lookin' as your average pupper. But this is Prohibition, boys and girls, and drastic times call for drastic measures. Where it’s Less Crowded is the easilyest speakable speakeasy you’ll ever find.

It is the year 1930, Chicago, USA. Ten years ago, Americans decided to end their streak of pulling off impossible feats and resolved to quit drinking, cold turkey. To absolutely no one’s surprise, it did not work. Thousands of “secret” bars flourished. Where it’s Less Crowded is one of them.

A casual, seemingly coincidental meeting in the busy street, empty small talk, “Let’s go Where it’s Less Crowded,” a knowing nod, and within ten minutes you are in Chicago’s best and cheapest speakeasy. And it’s run completely independently: no mafia bosses, no spies, no conspiracies--truly a place where you can speak easily.

Important Rules
This list is just a summary of the most important and/or unusual rules. For experienced players, simply reading this along with the setup tab will in most cases be sufficient.
  1. You may not edit or delete your posts, even for typos. Just post again.
  2. Each "day" phase in this game is 2 weeks in real life while "night" lasts 3 days.
  3. Do not post during night.
  4. When voting, you must format them correctly. If your vote isn't counted, there is a good chance you messed up the format. Incorrect capitalizations of usernames, as well as capital i/lowercase L, are not differentiated and will count as valid votes.
  5. Majority hang is off for Day 1, but it is on starting Day 2 until LYLO.
  6. Votes are not locked at LYLO.
  7. Do not OOT (Out of Thread) and talk about this game outside your role PM, QTs, or this thread, even if you are dead. A dead QT may be created upon request.
  8. This game is Dawn start, so Day 1 will not start until we make sure everyone knows the rules, speaking of which, if you've read all the rules below and are ready to start, say bang, pew, blam, kaboom, or some other guns-and-explosions-related onomatopoeia in the thread.
  9. All times will be given in Eastern Standard Time (EST, UTC -5) or Eastern Daylight Time (EDT, UTC -4), as applicable.
  10. Use common sense.
  11. All other rules are standard. However, if you break a rule that isn't covered here, you still liable to be warned.
  12. Best of luck, and hope you have fun!

  • Primary Rules
    Credits to Binomial-theorem, chaotic_iak, willwang123, and fdas amongst others for the rule template, as well as the Mafia Universe forum rules.

    • Make sure you are reasonably aware of all standard mafia game rules and follow them.
    • Do not post at night.
    • You may use your role PM to ask questions, such as rule clarifications or anything else.
    • Do not quote or pretend to quote any PMs from the mods, including role PMs.
    • Do not talk about this game outside of this topic to anyone other than the mods in charge, unless you are specifically given permission to do so.
    • Do not talk about other ongoing games in this thread.
    • You may not use any formatting that has the effect of concealing text. This includes size=1, hide="", color=#FFFFFF, and color=white, among others.
    • Hide tags are allowed as long as they conform to the above rule.
    • Do not edit or delete posts you have already submitted, even for things like typos: what goes in the thread stays in the thread. Instead, use EBWOP or EBWON as necessary.
    • If you are dead, do not post anything else about the game anywhere. You may still spectate the game, but you may no longer post in this thread. A dead QT may be created upon request.
    • Do not use the colors red (#FF0000), blue (#0000FF), green (#008000), or anything that appears similar, as these colors are used for modding purposes.
    • Do not lie to the mods.
    • Do not abuse loopholes.

  • Supplementary Rules
    The rules below cover implicitly discouraged or forbidden situations that may require additional clarification. During the course of normal gameplay, you will most likely not encounter these situations; however, we will detail possible moderator responses so you know what to expect should such a situation arise.

    This section may be updated periodically, in which case a notice will be posted in the announcement section at the bottom of the OP and an entry will be made in the modlog and OP changelog.

    • Do not gamethrow.

    This includes: hard claiming as a mafia for no reason, outing your teammates, posting screenshots of Mafia QT
    This does not include: giving up, strategic suiciding, lolcatting, or self-voting

    You should play towards your win condition(s) to the best of your ability. Intentionally sabotaging the game out for any reason - such as to troll, out of a personal vendetta, or out of frustration - is prohibited. Examples may include hard claiming as a mafia member or hanging a conftown as town. Disciplinary action will differ based on context, but will likely be severe, ranging from a force modkill to a forum ban depending on how game-breaking the gamethrow was. Please note that only intentionally playing against your wincon and hurting your teammates is banned - you are otherwise allowed to play however you like, and examples such as giving up or making mistakes in good faith will not prompt moderator intervention.

    Important note: According to decisions made by forum moderators detailed in this post, anything that appears to be gamethrowing - whether real or fake - is not allowed. Any completely serious statement along the lines of "I am actually mafia and/or my partner is [player]" that appears to be made with the intent to gamethrow will result in an immediate modkill, even if your alignment is town or the player you listed is not your partner. As this subject requires nuanced knowledge of the difference between lolcatting, gamethrowing, and the like, we recommend avoiding this type of situation entirely until you have enough experience to walk the fine line between the two.

    • Publicly discussing player alignments based on V/LAs, replacements, warnings, force replacements, modkills, and other moderator interactions is discouraged.

    This includes: "[player] was probably force replaced for trying to PM their mafia partner."
    This does not include: discussing lurking or player activity in reference to their alignment

    Seriously discussing a player's alignment based off of their V/LAs, replacements, warnings, or other essential moderator communications is highly discouraged. Speculation, jokes, or hot takes about such events are okay, but if a prolonged discussion occurs in the thread, the moderators may step in and ask participants to stop. In addition, the moderators may ask players to not discuss or speculate on the reasons behind a warning, force-replacement, or modkill. Repeatedly refusing to listen to the moderators may result in an issued warning.

    • Any significant, game-related, and intentionally exclusive inter-player communication outside of moderator-sanctioned channels are prohibited.

    This includes: an encrypted file only certain players possess the key to, the use of cryptosystems, using encryption programs to hide messages in posts
    This does not include: leaving breadcrumbs, manually encoded/decoded ciphers, messages in the form of a public riddle, brief conversation in non-English languages

    Unless private day-communication is specifically enabled in the game, you are prohibited attempting to contact or communicate with another player/other players privately. This is an extension of the rule banning posts with concealed text: all game-related information should be visible and reasonably able to be accessed and/or analyzed by all players. If you do wish to establish a secret method of daytime communication with your teammates, it may be a good idea to run your idea by the moderator team, but even if you do not, the punishment will likely only be a warning or a request to cease using the method in question.

    • Avoiding referencing OGI (Out of Game Information) in reads or creating OGI situations that could be construed as alignment indicative is recommended.

    This includes: adding all game players to a quote-on-quote "unrelated" PM, "[player] is really active in their other games, but not this one. Are they lurking as maf?" or "[player] and [player] have been acting unusually friendly in [this unrelated forum], I bet they're actually the scumteam."
    This does not include: mentioning real life, online interactions, and other unrelated comments as long as they are alignment agnostic

    OGI (Out of Game Information) is exactly what it stands for - anything that happens outside of the game thread - and it is only problematic when it is used alignment-indicatively. The most important things to remember are these: a) do not publicly discuss a read based on OGI, aka. anything from outside the game thread, and b) try to avoid situations outside the game thread that could cause other players to suspect your alignment, town or mafia. Everything else is fine, and mentioning things that happened outside the thread or in real life in the game thread is also fine, but it must be alignment agnostic, or reasonably unable to be used to determine your alignment. The purpose of this is to preserve game integrity by minimizing outside influences on what happens in the game, and the moderators may situationally intervene to stop an OGI discussion or incident and ask participants to cease. Repeated failure to listen to the moderator team may result in a issued warning.

  • Votes and Hangs
    • This game is based on deadline hang. This means that the player with the most votes at the deadline will be hanged.
    • If two players have the same amount of votes when the deadline is reached, the player who reached that amount of votes first will be hanged.
    • Starting Day 2, if a player reaches a majority of votes, then that player will be hanged.
    • Upon reaching LYLO (Hang-or-Lose), deadline hang will be turned off. If a majority hang is not reached by the deadline, the day will end without a hang, which will result in an automatic win for the mafia team.
    • Post votes in bold font in the format "Vote: <insert player here>". Since AoPS does not distinguish between usernames with different capitalizations, the capitalization of the player name does not matter. For example, "Vote: fdas" and "Vote: fDAs" are acceptable votes, while "Vote: fdas", "Vote fdas", “vote: fdas, "Vote:fdas", "Vote: fads", "Vote: fdsa", and "Vote: fdas" are not.
    • Spell the username of the player you wish to vote for exactly and do not use nicknames. There is one exception to this; see below.
    • For the purpose of voting, l (lowercase L) and I (uppercase i) are identical.
    • Votes in hide, tip, or quote tags will not be counted.
    • Improperly made votes will not be counted under any circumstances. It does not matter if it is the end of the game hammer and everyone revealed their roles. For instance, do not reveal your roles and declare victory until the mods say the game is officially over. If you want to purposely fake vote, it is recommended that you give the mods a heads up in your role PM so it is not accidentally counted.
    • You may vote for a no hang to occur during day. Vote this by saying "Vote: No Hang".
    • If there is an even number of players, half of the players voting for no hang is considered a majority. This applies only to a no hang.
    • You do not have to unvote before making a new vote, but if you wish to, unvote in the format "Unvote". An unvote will undo your previous vote, if any.
    • The moment the deadline passes, no more votes will be counted even if the mod has not made a post, and the game will proceed into twilight.
    • During twilight, players may still post. However, no more votes and/or unvotes will be processed.
    • Once the end-of-day scene is posted, the person hanged is officially dead and their role will be revealed. Night then immediately begins. You may not post during night.

  • Activity Rules
    • All times and deadlines will be given in times currently observed on the U.S. East Coast, namely Eastern Standard Time (EST, UTC -5) or Eastern Daylight Time (EDT, UTC -4) as applicable, unless otherwise noted.
    • Each day will be 336 hours (14 days). The moderators may extend the deadline for the end of the day at their discretion. Note that this will be unlikely and will usually only happen in circumstances of extreme inactivity or replacements too close to end of day.
    • The deadline will occur the very second 336 hours has occurred.
    • The night deadline will be 72 hours (3 days) after night begins.
    • When the night deadline is met, if the moderator does not receive a night action from either a player or their partner(s) (if they have them), the player will lose their chance to perform an action during that night.
    • Any night results will be sent after the night deadline
    • Night will end some time after the night deadline when the mods make the post announcing the end of night. You may not post until the moderators say you can, even if the night deadline has passed.
    • If a player does not make a game-advancing post in a 48-hour period and they are not in V/LA (Vacation/Long Absence), they will receive an in-game prod.
    • If a player does not make a game-advancing post in another 24-hour period after receiving an in-game prod and they are not in V/LA, they will receive a PM prod.
    • If a player does not make a game-advancing post in another 36-hour period after receiving a PM prod and they are not in V/LA, they will be replaced, unless they post before the moderators find a replacement.
    • If a player receives 5 PM Prods, they will be force replaced.
    • If a player is going to be in V/LA, they must post in bold in-thread or PM the mods. The moderators will not prod the player unless the V/LA is too long.

  • Player Etiquette
    • Do not use inappropriate language.
    • Do not make any personal attacks.
    • Do not post excessive spam.
    • Other behavior that disrupts the game for players and makes it harder for them to read and play may be a punishable offenses, at the mods' discretion.
    •  Follow all of the rules, including AoPS community rules.
    •  Be mature.
    • Use common sense.
    •  For a minor rule infraction, newbies can receive up to three warnings, SE’s up to two, and IC’s one. If a player commits an infraction after they have run out of warnings, or commits a modkill-level infraction, they will be modkilled or force-replaced at the mods' discretion. Please do not do anything bad enough to deserve a modkill.

    In addition to these guidelines, please note the category of posts which are implicitly discouraged by the Mafia! community. If certain criteria are met, players are now liable to be warned for these posts. Please note that this should have little to no effect on normal player behavior: this enforcement policy has been created to better define the "do not abuse loopholes" rule and to discourage negatively received posts. Please refer to the following tab for details.

    Discouraged Player Conduct
    The following are examples of posts that may result in a warning:

    • Posts with formatting that imitates the text indicating that a post has been edited.
    • Posts that are blank or otherwise contain no content.
    • Unsolicited advertising.
    • Multiple posts with identical content.
    • Posts containing large blocks of unoriginal text.
    • Extended serious communication in code or non-English languages.

    This list may be updated periodically, in which case a notice will be posted in the announcement section at the bottom of the OP and an entry will be made in the modlog and OP changelog.

    Posts in this category typically will not receive a warning unless one or more of the following criteria are met:

    1. The player possesses a history of warnings and/or disruptive behavior in this game
    2. The posts are excessive in number, scale, or objectionable content
    3. The posts have caused notably negative reactions from other players

    Again, the majority of players should rarely, if ever, have trouble with these guidelines or receive a warning even if they break them. This is simply to better and more transparently enforce the anti-loophole rule and to reduce deliberately provoking behaviors that are not explicitly forbidden by previous rules.

  • Moderator Information
    • mathjay and HannahW are the moderators of Mafia Game 209.
    • Red-colored text (#FF0000) is used for votecounts, warnings, and other official announcements by the moderators. It is also used for mafia flavor-related posts.
    • Blue-colored text (#0000FF) is used for activity checks, confirm counts, and other non-essential posts by the moderators.
    • Green-colored text (#00FF00) is used for town flavor-related posts.
    • Custom shades of these colors may be used for aesthetic purposes.
    • Current custom shades are: Town flavor green (#00f752), Activity Check blue (#005dff), mathjay personal blue (#4a86e8), HannahW personal blue (#0b5394).
    • The not voting section is to be ignored if it contradicts any other part of the votecount.
    • If an error to the votecount is not pointed out within 5 days, the incorrect votecount will become official.
    • The moderators may change the rules at any time, but major rule changes will still either be announced at the bottom of the OP as well as noted in the modlog and OP changelog. Most rule changes will be designed to have as little effect on normal, day-to-day play as possible and will instead be designed to streamline gameplay. However, it is recommended that players occasionally update themselves on rule changes.
    • The moderators have the last say in interpreting the rules.

    MG209 OP is based on mathjay formatted ruleset (MFR) version 3.
    Current version: 3.0
    Version History

    Detailed version history does not exist because of retroactive version designation.
Setup
This game is based on the Matrix6 setup. This is a semi-open setup.

In this setup, there are 9 players. Before the game starts, the setup will be randomly chosen from 6 possible role combinations:
  • Setup 1: 1 Town Jailkeeper, 6 Vanilla Townies, 2 Mafia Goons
  • Setup 2: 1 Town Cop, 1 Town Doctor, 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Mafia Roleblocker, 1 Mafia Goon
  • Setup 3: 1 Town Tracker, 1 1-shot Bulletproof Townie, 5 Vanilla Townies, 2 Mafia Goons
  • Setup A: 1 Town Jailkeeper, 1 1-shot Bulletproof Townie, 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Mafia Roleblocker, 1 Mafia Goon
  • Setup B: 1 Town Cop, 6 Vanilla Townies, 2 Mafia Goons
  • Setup C: 1 Town Doctor, 1 Town Tracker, 5 Vanilla Townies, 2 Mafia Goons
There are a couple other setup mechanics that should be clarified:
  • This game is Dawn start. Dawn will end once everyone confirms, upon which it will be Day 1 and the deadline will be 14 days from then.
  • Doctors cannot protect themselves two nights in a row.
  • If applicable, a Mafia Roleblocker's action takes precedence over a Town Jailkeeper's action.
  • A Mafia Roleblocker can kill and roleblock in the same night.
  • Cop results are sane.
  • Votes are not locked at LYLO.
  • Mafia do not have daychat and may only discuss in the mafia Quicktopic during night.

Role PMs
Sloppy Joe (Vanilla Townie) wrote:
Hello, [player]!

You are a Sloppy Joe (Vanilla Townie). You're an average, hardworking American, just tryna make do, and you work in construction during the day. During the night though, if you just so happen to like going to Where it's Less Crowded to socialize with your fellow average Americans... well, it's nobody's business, is it? After all, a bit of booze is good for the complexion.

Your power lies in your powers of observation, your voice, and your vote.

The game thread can be found here.

You win when all Mafia are killed, so your goal should be to find them. Play towards your win condition, and good luck!
Veteran (Town Doctor) wrote:
Hello, [player]!

You are the Veteran (Town Doctor). You fought in the Great War, slogging your way through the trenches to battle in the front lines, and you managed to come back home in one piece. More or less. It's already been over 10 years since the war ended, but some things, you really have trouble forgetting. Nothing a bottle or two can't fix, but... still unfortunate. But eh, what can you do?

Because you are a veteran of war, you have a small first aid you carry around with you all the time; it's enough to stitch up a couple bullet wounds, something all too common in the city. Every night, you may PM me the name of a player. You will then stay by that person during the night, ready to operate on them should they get shot, protecting them and preventing them from dying. You can also target yourself, but you cannot do this two nights in a row.

The game thread can be found here.

You win when all Mafia are killed, so your goal should be to find them. Play towards your win condition, and good luck!
Insomniac (Town Tracker) wrote:
Hello, [player]!

You are the Insomniac (Town Tracker). You often have trouble sleeping because your thoughts refuse to leave you alone, so you wander the city streets in the nighttime. And if you just so happen to wander to Where it's Less Crowded... I mean, if a shot or two of fireball makes you warm and drowsy inside, ya gotta take what you can get in this world, ya know?

Because you have insomnia, you lie awake each night and are able to hear the soft footsteps of people sneaking around. Every night, you may PM me the name of a player. You will then listen for that person's footsteps during the night, tracking them, and will be informed of who that player visited or targeted, if any.

The game thread can be found here.

You win when all Mafia are killed, so your goal should be to find them. Play towards your win condition, and good luck!
Dancer (Town Jailkeeper) wrote:
Hello, [player]!

You are the Dancer (Town Jailkeeper). You flit between gigs, always moving, always busy, because a dancer's career is fleeting. It's a good life, although sometimes you do wonder what you'll do once you're too old to dance like you do now. You don't dwell on it, though, especially because you just finished your last job of the day and you're ready to have some fun. Maybe you'll head on over to Where it's Less Crowded?

Because you are a dancer, you know how to use your words and gestures to captivate people and distract them into drinking a bit more they should have. Every night, you may PM me the name of a player. You will then charm them until they are too inebriated to do much other than sleep. You will also stand guard over them during the night, making sure no one harms them. Any night actions your target attempts to perform - including the mafia factional kill - will not resolve, and they will be unable to die for that night. You cannot target yourself with this ability.

The game thread can be found here.

You win when all Mafia are killed, so your goal should be to find them. Play towards your win condition, and good luck!
Psychic (Town Cop) wrote:
Hello, [player]!

You are the Psychic (Town Cop). A lot of people think you're crazy, which is funny because you think a lot of the people who come to you for their fortunes are crazy. The sort of gossip you've heard over the years would make the tabloids drool. You'll take those secrets to your grave, of course - you have far too much class and common sense to kiss and tell - but sometimes, you have to head down to Where it's Less Crowded, sit down with a glass, and just say to yourself, "What on earth did I just hear?"

Because you are a psychic, you can tell people's fortunes from their palms and tea leaves, reading their spiritual energy. Every night, you may PM me the name of a player. You will then secretly read their alignment and will be informed of whether they are Town or Mafia. You may also receive a "No result" if your action is prevented from resolving.

The game thread can be found here.

You win when all Mafia are killed, so your goal should be to find them. Play towards your win condition, and good luck!
Bodyguard (1-shot Bulletproof Townie) wrote:
Hello, [player]!

You are the Bodyguard (1-shot Bulletproof Townie). Most of the time, your work as a bodyguard is quite boring: chauffeur your client to an important meeting, usher them into the building, stand guard outside the door, rinse and repeat. Nothing ever really happens, and to be honest to yourself, you like it that way. But if you just so happen to get a bit too tipsy at Where it's Less Crowded and loudly boast of how you survived a mob gunfight... well, thank God it's only a story, right?

Because you are a bodyguard, you still have your bulletproof vest on. It's a bit old, but it still has some life in it. You will survive a single shot, but after that, if you are shot again, you will be killed. You are also not immune to the daily hang, your ability cannot be roleblocked, and you do not know when you lose your bulletproof vest.

The game thread can be found here.

You win when all Mafia are killed, so your goal should be to find them. Play towards your win condition, and good luck!
Thug (Mafia Goon) wrote:
Hello, [player]!

You are a Thug (Mafia Goon). You're hired muscle; you intimidate people, waving around crowbars and guns for a living. It's a good life, maybe not the most spick-and-span, but still good. Your bosses are happy, the illicit liquor business run by the mob is booming, and things really couldn't get better. What's this you hear about something somewhere less crowded though? A bar? Cheap beer? Normally, you'd be excited, but you know your bosses won't be as happy. Might as well take care of the problem before they hear about it. Maybe you'll even get a raise out of this?

Your partner is [player], and you may discuss with them in this QuickTopic while you are alive. Together, you may submit a factional kill each night to eliminate a player.

The game thread can be found here.

You win when the Mafia are the only ones left, or if nothing can prevent this, so your goal should be to stay hidden and kill Town before they find and hang you. Play towards your win condition, and good luck!
Professional Thug (Mafia Roleblocker) wrote:
Hello, [player]!

You are a Professional Thug (Mafia Roleblocker). You're not sure where you lost your heart, but you kill and kidnap people for a living. You're good at your job, too - eliminating your targets quickly and discretely - and you get paid handsomely for it... which is why you're not quite sure why you're here, in a bar, helping your colleague in crime with his abysmally haphazard plan. It was a stupid idea, and you're obviously not gonna get paid, but maybe you can keep the situation from completely imploding into a catastrophe?

Because you are a professional, you have a bottle of chloroform with you to make sure you can pull off a clean job, ensuring that your target won't accidentally wake up for the night. Every night, you may PM me the name of a player. You will then roleblock that person for that night, preventing their night actions, if any, from resolving.

Your partner is [player], and you may discuss with them in this QuickTopic while you are alive. Together, you may submit a factional kill each night to eliminate a player. You are allowed to perform both the factional kill and your roleblock in a single night.

The game thread can be found here.

You win when the Mafia are the only ones left, or if nothing can prevent this, so your goal should be to stay hidden and kill Town before they find and hang you. Play towards your win condition, and good luck!

Clarification for OOT Rules
Referring to anything that has happened in an ongoing game, even if you do not say which game it is, is OOT.
For example:
"I have reasons but they're OOT" is not OOT.
"I'm scumreading him 'cuz he did the same thing in an ongoing game" is a bit more iffy.
"He fakeclaimed in [ongoing]" however, is definitely OOT and will be warned.
Please wait until said ongoing games are over before you use information from those games as part of your attacks and/or defenses.

post_canonical:
[center][size=200]Welcome to Mafia Game 209[/size]
[size=150]Newbie Game 84[/center]
$ $
[center][size=150][b]Less Crowded Mafia[/b][/size][/center]

[i]Welcome to Where it’s Less Crowded: a place with a name that, if you remove the capital letters, is as innocent-lookin' as your average pupper. But this is Prohibition, boys and girls, and drastic times call for drastic measures. Where it’s Less Crowded is the easilyest speakable speakeasy you’ll ever find. 

It is the year 1930, Chicago, USA. Ten years ago, Americans decided to end their streak of pulling off impossible feats and resolved to quit drinking, cold turkey. To absolutely no one’s surprise, it did not work. Thousands of “secret” bars flourished. Where it’s Less Crowded is one of them. 

A casual, seemingly coincidental meeting in the busy street, empty small talk, “Let’s go Where it’s Less Crowded,” a knowing nod, and within ten minutes you are in Chicago’s best and cheapest speakeasy. And it’s run completely independently: no mafia bosses, no spies, no conspiracies--truly a place where you can speak easily.[/i]

[hide=Important Rules]
This list is just a summary of the most important and/or unusual rules. For experienced players, simply reading this along with the setup tab will in most cases be sufficient. 
[list=1][*] You may not [b]edit or delete[/b] your posts, even for typos. Just post again. 
[*] Each "day" phase in this game is [b]2 weeks in real life[/b] while "night" lasts [b]3 days[/b].
[*] [b]Do not post during night.[/b]
[*] When voting, you [b]must format them correctly[/b]. If your vote isn't counted, there is a good chance you messed up the format. Incorrect capitalizations of usernames, as well as capital i/lowercase L, are not differentiated and will count as valid votes. 
[*] [b]Majority hang is off[/b] for Day 1, but it is [b]on[/b] starting Day 2 until LYLO.
[*] Votes are [b]not locked at LYLO.[/b] 
[*] [b]Do not OOT (Out of Thread)[/b] and talk about this game outside your role PM, QTs, or this thread, even if you are dead. A dead QT may be created upon request. 
[*] This game is [b]Dawn start,[/b] so Day 1 will not start until we make sure everyone knows the rules, speaking of which, if you've read all the rules below and are ready to start, say bang, pew, blam, kaboom, or some other guns-and-explosions-related onomatopoeia in the thread. 
[*] All [b]times[/b] will be given in [b]Eastern Standard Time[/b] (EST, UTC -5) or Eastern Daylight Time (EDT, UTC -4), as applicable. 
[*] [b]Use common sense.[/b] 
[*] [b]All other rules are standard.[/b] However, if you break a rule that isn't covered here, you still liable to be warned. 
[*] Best of luck, and hope you have fun! 
[/hide]

[list][*][hide=Primary Rules]
Credits to Binomial-theorem, chaotic_iak, willwang123, and fdas amongst others for the rule template, as well as the [url=https://www.mafiauniverse.com/forums/threads/27-Forum-Rules]Mafia Universe forum rules[/url]. 


[list]
[*] Make sure you are reasonably aware of all standard mafia game rules and follow them. 
[*] Do not post at night.
[*] You may use your role PM to ask questions, such as rule clarifications or anything else.
[*] Do not quote or pretend to quote any PMs from the mods, including role PMs.
[*] Do not talk about this game outside of this topic to anyone other than the mods in charge, unless you are specifically given permission to do so. 
[*] Do not talk about other ongoing games in this thread.
[*] You may not use any formatting that has the effect of concealing text. This includes size=1, hide="", color=#FFFFFF, and color=white, among others. 
[*] Hide tags are allowed as long as they conform to the above rule.
[*] Do not edit or delete posts you have already submitted, even for things like typos: what goes in the thread stays in the thread. Instead, use EBWOP or EBWON as necessary. 
[*] If you are dead, do not post anything else about the game anywhere. You may still spectate the game, but you may no longer post in this thread. A dead QT may be created upon request. 
[*] Do not use the colors red (#FF0000), blue (#0000FF), green (#008000), or anything that appears similar, as these colors are used for modding purposes. 
[*] Do not lie to the mods. 
[*] Do not abuse loopholes.[/list]
[/hide]

[*][hide=Supplementary Rules]
The rules below cover implicitly discouraged or forbidden situations that may require additional clarification. During the course of normal gameplay, you will most likely not encounter these situations; however, we will detail possible moderator responses so you know what to expect should such a situation arise. 

This section may be updated periodically, in which case a notice will be posted in the announcement section at the bottom of the OP and an entry will be made in the modlog and OP changelog. 


[list][*] Do not gamethrow.[/list]

[b]This includes:[/b] hard claiming as a mafia for no reason, outing your teammates, posting screenshots of Mafia QT
[b]This does not include:[/b] giving up, strategic suiciding, lolcatting, or self-voting

You should play towards your win condition(s) to the best of your ability. Intentionally sabotaging the game out for any reason - such as to troll, out of a personal vendetta, or out of frustration - is prohibited. Examples may include hard claiming as a mafia member or hanging a conftown as town. Disciplinary action will differ based on context, but will likely be severe, ranging from a force modkill to a forum ban depending on how game-breaking the gamethrow was. Please note that only intentionally playing against your wincon and hurting your teammates is banned - you are otherwise allowed to play however you like, and examples such as giving up or making mistakes in good faith will not prompt moderator intervention. 

[b]Important note:[/b] According to decisions made by forum moderators [url=https://aops.com/community/p15771679]detailed in this post[/url], anything that appears to be gamethrowing - whether real or fake - is not allowed. Any completely serious statement along the lines of "I am actually mafia and/or my partner is [player]" that appears to be made with the intent to gamethrow will result in an immediate modkill, even if your alignment is town or the player you listed is not your partner. As this subject requires nuanced knowledge of the difference between lolcatting, gamethrowing, and the like, we recommend avoiding this type of situation entirely until you have enough experience to walk the fine line between the two. 


[list][*] Publicly discussing player alignments based on V/LAs, replacements, warnings, force replacements, modkills, and other moderator interactions is discouraged.[/list]

[b]This includes:[/b] "[player] was probably force replaced for trying to PM their mafia partner."
[b]This does not include:[/b] discussing lurking or player activity in reference to their alignment

Seriously discussing a player's alignment based off of their V/LAs, replacements, warnings, or other essential moderator communications is highly discouraged. Speculation, jokes, or hot takes about such events are okay, but if a prolonged discussion occurs in the thread, the moderators may step in and ask participants to stop. In addition, the moderators may ask players to not discuss or speculate on the reasons behind a warning, force-replacement, or modkill. Repeatedly refusing to listen to the moderators may result in an issued warning. 


[list][*] Any significant, game-related, and intentionally exclusive inter-player communication outside of moderator-sanctioned channels are prohibited. [/list]

[b]This includes:[/b] an encrypted file only certain players possess the key to, the use of cryptosystems, using encryption programs to hide messages in posts
[b]This does not include:[/b] leaving breadcrumbs, manually encoded/decoded ciphers, messages in the form of a public riddle, brief conversation in non-English languages

Unless private day-communication is specifically enabled in the game, you are prohibited attempting to contact or communicate with another player/other players privately. This is an extension of the rule banning posts with concealed text: all game-related information should be visible and reasonably able to be accessed and/or analyzed by all players. If you do wish to establish a secret method of daytime communication with your teammates, it may be a good idea to run your idea by the moderator team, but even if you do not, the punishment will likely only be a warning or a request to cease using the method in question. 


[list][*] Avoiding referencing OGI (Out of Game Information) in reads or creating OGI situations that could be construed as alignment indicative is recommended. [/list]

[b]This includes:[/b] adding all game players to a quote-on-quote "unrelated" PM, "[player] is really active in their other games, but not this one. Are they lurking as maf?" or "[player] and [player] have been acting unusually friendly in [this unrelated forum], I bet they're actually the scumteam."
[b]This does not include:[/b] mentioning real life, online interactions, and other unrelated comments as long as they are alignment agnostic

OGI (Out of Game Information) is exactly what it stands for - anything that happens outside of the game thread - and it is only problematic when it is used alignment-indicatively. The most important things to remember are these: a) do not publicly discuss a read based on OGI, aka. anything from outside the game thread, and b) try to avoid situations outside the game thread that could cause other players to suspect your alignment, town or mafia. Everything else is fine, and mentioning things that happened outside the thread or in real life in the game thread is also fine, but it must be alignment agnostic, or reasonably unable to be used to determine your alignment. The purpose of this is to preserve game integrity by minimizing outside influences on what happens in the game, and the moderators may situationally intervene to stop an OGI discussion or incident and ask participants to cease. Repeated failure to listen to the moderator team may result in a issued warning. 
[/hide]

[*][hide=Votes and Hangs]
[list]
[*] This game is based on deadline hang. This means that the player with the most votes at the deadline will be hanged.
[*] If two players have the same amount of votes when the deadline is reached, the player who reached that amount of votes first will be hanged.
[*] Starting Day 2, if a player reaches a majority of votes, then that player will be hanged. 
[*] Upon reaching LYLO (Hang-or-Lose), deadline hang will be turned off. If a majority hang is not reached by the deadline, the day will end without a hang, which will result in an automatic win for the mafia team. 
[*] Post votes in bold font in the format "[b]Vote: <insert player here>[/b]". Since AoPS does not distinguish between usernames with different capitalizations, the capitalization of the player name does not matter. For example, "[b]Vote: fdas[/b]" and "[b]Vote: fDAs[/b]" are acceptable votes, while "Vote: [b]fdas[/b]", "[b]Vote fdas[/b]", “[b]vote: fdas[/b], "[b]Vote:fdas[/b]", "[b]Vote: fads[/b]", "[b]Vote: fdsa[/b]", and "[u]Vote: fdas[/u]" are not.
[*] Spell the username of the player you wish to vote for exactly and do not use nicknames. There is one exception to this; see below. 
[*] For the purpose of voting, l (lowercase L) and I (uppercase i) are identical.
[*] Votes in hide, tip, or quote tags will not be counted.
[*] Improperly made votes will not be counted under any circumstances. It does not matter if it is the end of the game hammer and everyone revealed their roles. For instance, do not reveal your roles and declare victory until the mods say the game is officially over. If you want to purposely fake vote, it is recommended that you give the mods a heads up in your role PM so it is not accidentally counted.
[*] You may vote for a no hang to occur during day. Vote this by saying "[b]Vote: No Hang[/b]".
[*] If there is an even number of players, half of the players voting for no hang is considered a majority. This applies only to a no hang. 
[*] You do not have to unvote before making a new vote, but if you wish to, unvote in the format "[b]Unvote[/b]". An unvote will undo your previous vote, if any.
[*] The moment the deadline passes, no more votes will be counted even if the mod has not made a post, and the game will proceed into twilight. 
[*] During twilight, players may still post. However, no more votes and/or unvotes will be processed.
[*] Once the end-of-day scene is posted, the person hanged is officially dead and their role will be revealed. Night then immediately begins. You may not post during night.[/list]
[/hide]

[*][hide=Activity Rules]
[list]
[*] All times and deadlines will be given in times currently observed on the U.S. East Coast, namely Eastern Standard Time (EST, UTC -5) or Eastern Daylight Time (EDT, UTC -4) as applicable, unless otherwise noted.
[*] Each day will be 336 hours (14 days). The moderators may extend the deadline for the end of the day at their discretion. Note that this will be unlikely and will usually only happen in circumstances of extreme inactivity or replacements too close to end of day. 
[*] The deadline will occur the very second 336 hours has occurred.
[*] The night deadline will be 72 hours (3 days) after night begins.
[*] When the night deadline is met, if the moderator does not receive a night action from either a player or their partner(s) (if they have them), the player will lose their chance to perform an action during that night. 
[*] Any night results will be sent after the night deadline
[*] Night will end some time after the night deadline when the mods make the post announcing the end of night. You may not post until the moderators say you can, even if the night deadline has passed.
[*] If a player does not make a game-advancing post in a 48-hour period and they are not in V/LA (Vacation/Long Absence), they will receive an in-game prod.
[*] If a player does not make a game-advancing post in another 24-hour period after receiving an in-game prod and they are not in V/LA, they will receive a PM prod.
[*] If a player does not make a game-advancing post in another 36-hour period after receiving a PM prod and they are not in V/LA, they will be replaced, unless they post before the moderators find a replacement.
[*] If a player receives 5 PM Prods, they will be force replaced.
[*] If a player is going to be in V/LA, they must post in [b]bold[/b] in-thread or PM the mods. The moderators will not prod the player unless the V/LA is too long.[list]
[/hide]

[*][hide=Player Etiquette]
[list]
[*] Do not use inappropriate language.
[*] Do not make any personal attacks.
[*] Do not post excessive spam. 
[*] Other behavior that disrupts the game for players and makes it harder for them to read and play may be a punishable offenses, at the mods' discretion. 
[*] Follow all of the rules, including AoPS community rules. 
[*] Be mature.
[*] Use common sense. 
[*] For a minor rule infraction, newbies can receive up to three warnings, SE’s up to two, and IC’s one. If a player commits an infraction after they have run out of warnings, or commits a modkill-level infraction, they will be modkilled or force-replaced at the mods' discretion. Please do not do anything bad enough to deserve a modkill.[/list]

In addition to these guidelines, please note the category of posts which are implicitly discouraged by the Mafia! community. If certain criteria are met, players are now liable to be warned for these posts. Please note that this should have little to no effect on normal player behavior: this enforcement policy has been created to better define the "do not abuse loopholes" rule and to discourage negatively received posts. Please refer to the following tab for details. 

[hide=Discouraged Player Conduct]
The following are examples of posts that may result in a warning: 


[list]
[*] Posts with formatting that imitates the text indicating that a post has been edited. 
[*] Posts that are blank or otherwise contain no content. 
[*] Unsolicited advertising. 
[*] Multiple posts with identical content. 
[*] Posts containing large blocks of unoriginal text. 
[*] Extended serious communication in code or non-English languages. 
[/list]

This list may be updated periodically, in which case a notice will be posted in the announcement section at the bottom of the OP and an entry will be made in the modlog and OP changelog. 

Posts in this category typically will not receive a warning unless one or more of the following criteria are met: 


[list=1]
[*] The player possesses a history of warnings and/or disruptive behavior in this game
[*] The posts are excessive in number, scale, or objectionable content
[*] The posts have caused notably negative reactions from other players
[/list]

Again, the majority of players should rarely, if ever, have trouble with these guidelines or receive a warning even if they break them. This is simply to better and more transparently enforce the anti-loophole rule and to reduce deliberately provoking behaviors that are not explicitly forbidden by previous rules.
[/hide]
[/hide]

[*][hide=Moderator Information]
[list]
[*] mathjay and HannahW are the moderators of Mafia Game 209. 
[*] [color=#FF0000]Red-colored text (#FF0000)[/color] is used for votecounts, warnings, and other official announcements by the moderators. It is also used for mafia flavor-related posts.
[*] [color=#0000FF]Blue-colored text (#0000FF)[/color] is used for activity checks, confirm counts, and other non-essential posts by the moderators. 
[*] [color=#00FF00]Green-colored text (#00FF00)[/color] is used for town flavor-related posts.
[*] Custom shades of these colors may be used for aesthetic purposes. 
[*] Current custom shades are: Town flavor green ([color=#00f752]#00f752[/color]), Activity Check blue ([color=#005dff]#005dff[/color]), mathjay personal blue ([color=#4a86e8]#4a86e8[/color]), HannahW personal blue ([color=#0b5394]#0b5394[/color]).
[*] The not voting section is to be ignored if it contradicts any other part of the votecount.
[*] If an error to the votecount is not pointed out within 5 days, the incorrect votecount will become official.
[*] The moderators may change the rules at any time, but major rule changes will still either be announced at the bottom of the OP as well as noted in the modlog and OP changelog. Most rule changes will be designed to have as little effect on normal, day-to-day play as possible and will instead be designed to streamline gameplay. However, it is recommended that players occasionally update themselves on rule changes. 
[*] The moderators have the last say in interpreting the rules. [/list]

MG209 OP is based on mathjay formatted ruleset (MFR) version 3. 
Current version: 3.0

[hide=Setup]
This game is based on the [url=https://wiki.mafiascum.net/index.php?title=Matrix6]Matrix6[/url] setup. This is a semi-open setup.

In this setup, there are 9 players. Before the game starts, the setup will be randomly chosen from 6 possible role combinations:
[list][*] [b]Setup 1:[/b] 1 Town Jailkeeper, 6 Vanilla Townies, 2 Mafia Goons
[*] [b]Setup 2:[/b] 1 Town Cop, 1 Town Doctor, 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Mafia Roleblocker, 1 Mafia Goon
[*] [b]Setup 3:[/b] 1 Town Tracker, 1 1-shot Bulletproof Townie, 5 Vanilla Townies, 2 Mafia Goons
[*] [b]Setup A:[/b] 1 Town Jailkeeper, 1 1-shot Bulletproof Townie, 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Mafia Roleblocker, 1 Mafia Goon
[*] [b]Setup B:[/b] 1 Town Cop, 6 Vanilla Townies, 2 Mafia Goons
[*] [b]Setup C:[/b] 1 Town Doctor, 1 Town Tracker, 5 Vanilla Townies, 2 Mafia Goons[/list]
There are a couple other setup mechanics that should be clarified: 
[list][*] This game is Dawn start. Dawn will end once everyone confirms, upon which it will be Day 1 and the deadline will be 14 days from then. 
[*] Doctors cannot protect themselves two nights in a row.
[*] If applicable, a Mafia Roleblocker's action takes precedence over a Town Jailkeeper's action.
[*] A Mafia Roleblocker can kill and roleblock in the same night.
[*] Cop results are sane.
[*] Votes are not locked at LYLO. 
[*] Mafia do not have daychat and may only discuss in the mafia Quicktopic during night.[/list]

[hide=Role PMs]
[quote=Sloppy Joe (Vanilla Townie)]
Hello, [player]!

You are a [color=#00f752]Sloppy Joe (Vanilla Townie)[/color]. You're an average, hardworking American, just tryna make do, and you work in construction during the day. During the night though, if you just so happen to like going to Where it's Less Crowded to socialize with your fellow average Americans... well, it's nobody's business, is it? After all, a bit of booze is good for the complexion. 

Your power lies in your powers of observation, your voice, and your vote. 

The game thread can be found [url=https://i.ibb.co/Mg0nt5m/defenestration.gif]here[/url].

You win when all Mafia are killed, so your goal should be to find them. Play towards your win condition, and good luck!
[/quote] 

[quote=Veteran (Town Doctor)]
Hello, [player]!

You are the [color=#00f752]Veteran (Town Doctor)[/color]. You fought in the Great War, slogging your way through the trenches to battle in the front lines, and you managed to come back home in one piece. More or less. It's already been over 10 years since the war ended, but some things, you really have trouble forgetting. Nothing a bottle or two can't fix, but... still unfortunate. But eh, what can you do?

Because you are a veteran of war, you have a small first aid you carry around with you all the time; it's enough to stitch up a couple bullet wounds, something all too common in the city. Every night, you may PM me the name of a player. You will then stay by that person during the night, ready to operate on them should they get shot, protecting them and preventing them from dying. You can also target yourself, but you cannot do this two nights in a row. 

The game thread can be found [url=https://i.ibb.co/Mg0nt5m/defenestration.gif]here[/url].

You win when all Mafia are killed, so your goal should be to find them. Play towards your win condition, and good luck!
[/quote]

[quote=Insomniac (Town Tracker)]
Hello, [player]!

You are the [color=#00f752]Insomniac (Town Tracker)[/color]. You often have trouble sleeping because your thoughts refuse to leave you alone, so you wander the city streets in the nighttime. And if you just so happen to wander to Where it's Less Crowded... I mean, if a shot or two of fireball makes you warm and drowsy inside, ya gotta take what you can get in this world, ya know? 

Because you have insomnia, you lie awake each night and are able to hear the soft footsteps of people sneaking around. Every night, you may PM me the name of a player. You will then listen for that person's footsteps during the night, tracking them, and will be informed of who that player visited or targeted, if any. 

The game thread can be found [url=https://i.ibb.co/Mg0nt5m/defenestration.gif]here[/url].

You win when all Mafia are killed, so your goal should be to find them. Play towards your win condition, and good luck!
[/quote]

[quote=Dancer (Town Jailkeeper)]
Hello, [player]!

You are the [color=#00f752]Dancer (Town Jailkeeper)[/color]. You flit between gigs, always moving, always busy, because a dancer's career is fleeting. It's a good life, although sometimes you do wonder what you'll do once you're too old to dance like you do now. You don't dwell on it, though, especially because you just finished your last job of the day and you're ready to have some fun. Maybe you'll head on over to Where it's Less Crowded? 

Because you are a dancer, you know how to use your words and gestures to captivate people and distract them into drinking a bit more they should have. Every night, you may PM me the name of a player. You will then charm them until they are too inebriated to do much other than sleep. You will also stand guard over them during the night, making sure no one harms them. Any night actions your target attempts to perform - including the mafia factional kill - will not resolve, and they will be unable to die for that night. You cannot target yourself with this ability. 

The game thread can be found [url=https://i.ibb.co/Mg0nt5m/defenestration.gif]here[/url].

You win when all Mafia are killed, so your goal should be to find them. Play towards your win condition, and good luck!
[/quote]

[quote=Psychic (Town Cop)]
Hello, [player]!

You are the [color=#00f752]Psychic (Town Cop)[/color]. A lot of people think you're crazy, which is funny because you think a lot of the people who come to you for their fortunes are crazy. The sort of gossip you've heard over the years would make the tabloids drool. You'll take those secrets to your grave, of course - you have far too much class and common sense to kiss and tell - but sometimes, you have to head down to Where it's Less Crowded, sit down with a glass, and just say to yourself, "What on earth did I just hear?" 

Because you are a psychic, you can tell people's fortunes from their palms and tea leaves, reading their spiritual energy. Every night, you may PM me the name of a player. You will then secretly read their alignment and will be informed of whether they are Town or Mafia. You may also receive a "No result" if your action is prevented from resolving. 

The game thread can be found [url=https://i.ibb.co/Mg0nt5m/defenestration.gif]here[/url].

You win when all Mafia are killed, so your goal should be to find them. Play towards your win condition, and good luck!
[/quote]

[quote=Bodyguard (1-shot Bulletproof Townie)]
Hello, [player]!

You are the [color=#00f752]Bodyguard (1-shot Bulletproof Townie)[/color]. Most of the time, your work as a bodyguard is quite boring: chauffeur your client to an important meeting, usher them into the building, stand guard outside the door, rinse and repeat. Nothing ever really happens, and to be honest to yourself, you like it that way. But if you just so happen to get a bit too tipsy at Where it's Less Crowded and loudly boast of how you survived a mob gunfight... well, thank God it's only a story, right? 

Because you are a bodyguard, you still have your bulletproof vest on. It's a bit old, but it still has some life in it. You will survive a single shot, but after that, if you are shot again, you will be killed. You are also not immune to the daily hang, your ability cannot be roleblocked, and you do not know when you lose your bulletproof vest. 

The game thread can be found [url=https://i.ibb.co/Mg0nt5m/defenestration.gif]here[/url].

You win when all Mafia are killed, so your goal should be to find them. Play towards your win condition, and good luck!
[/quote]

[quote=Thug (Mafia Goon)]
Hello, [player]!

You are a [color=red]Thug (Mafia Goon)[/color]. You're hired muscle; you intimidate people, waving around crowbars and guns for a living. It's a good life, maybe not the most spick-and-span, but still good. Your bosses are happy, the illicit liquor business run by the mob is booming, and things really couldn't get better. What's this you hear about something somewhere less crowded though? A bar? Cheap beer? Normally, you'd be excited, but you know your bosses won't be as happy. Might as well take care of the problem before they hear about it. Maybe you'll even get a raise out of this? 

Your partner is [player], and you may discuss with them in [url=http://bitly.com/98K8eH]this QuickTopic[/url] while you are alive. Together, you may submit a factional kill each night to eliminate a player. 

The game thread can be found [url=https://i.ibb.co/Mg0nt5m/defenestration.gif]here[/url].

You win when the Mafia are the only ones left, or if nothing can prevent this, so your goal should be to stay hidden and kill Town before they find and hang you. Play towards your win condition, and good luck!
[/quote]

[quote=Professional Thug (Mafia Roleblocker)]
Hello, [player]!

You are a [color=red]Professional Thug (Mafia Roleblocker)[/color]. You're not sure where you lost your heart, but you kill and kidnap people for a living. You're good at your job, too - eliminating your targets quickly and discretely - and you get paid handsomely for it... which is why you're not quite sure why you're here, in a bar, helping your colleague in crime with his abysmally haphazard plan. It was a stupid idea, and you're obviously not gonna get paid, but maybe you can keep the situation from completely imploding into a catastrophe?

Because you are a professional, you have a bottle of chloroform with you to make sure you can pull off a clean job, ensuring that your target won't accidentally wake up for the night. Every night, you may PM me the name of a player. You will then roleblock that person for that night, preventing their night actions, if any, from resolving. 

Your partner is [player], and you may discuss with them in [url=http://bitly.com/98K8eH]this QuickTopic[/url] while you are alive. Together, you may submit a factional kill each night to eliminate a player. You are allowed to perform both the factional kill and your roleblock in a single night. 

The game thread can be found [url=https://i.ibb.co/Mg0nt5m/defenestration.gif]here[/url].

You win when the Mafia are the only ones left, or if nothing can prevent this, so your goal should be to stay hidden and kill Town before they find and hang you. Play towards your win condition, and good luck!

[hide=Clarification for OOT Rules]
Referring to anything that has happened in an ongoing game, even if you do not say which game it is, is OOT.
For example: 
"I have reasons but they're OOT" is not OOT.
"I'm scumreading him 'cuz he did the same thing in an ongoing game" is a bit more iffy. 
"He fakeclaimed in [ongoing]" however, is definitely OOT and will be warned. 
Please wait until said ongoing games are over before you use information from those games as part of your attacks and/or defenses.

Hello all! I am one of your two SEs for this game. Let's start with a bang and hop straight into RVS (Random Voting Stage). Vote: TheMagician as per instructions from random.org.

Ah that felt good. My first RVS post in years.

For those unaware, RVS is an early stage of the game where people... vote... randomly. The goal is to create some kind of reaction, from which discussion can start, and votes can become not random.

Also, the #1 thing you can do to improve your gameplay besides playing games is reading other games. I'm sure the FAQ has suggested games to read, but I will suggest game #187, simply because it's so short.

Someone else can do RQS, I'm not creative enough rn.

post_rendered:
I'm of the belief that no vote is random. And if someone explicitly says they are using some random number generator to vote, that in and of itself is a tell.

Vote: aroodman because using random.org is just that: completely random. No one can read into it, purely because it's random. There's literally no information to be gained from this.

RVS in the past I've seen is just sorta a voting frenzy, but these votes (usually) have some reasoning behind them, even if it's as weak as "oh you were mafia in your previous game lets pew pew you." Even if the information they give is weak, it's still some information. We can build off of it. I would like to see some more votes thrown around; there isn't a majority on Day 1 so we don't have to worry about someone dying early.

post_rendered:
Can't tell how serious you are. I was mainly just trying to introduce the concept, because it's a newbie game.

But yeah, hopefully once everyone confirms, this serves as a model for how discussion works. One person pops in with an accusation against another player, and said player must justify his or her own actions, or at least explain the thought process behind them. This generates more reactions, and things blow up from there. Discussion is best when it's explosive like this, and we want to avoid having it fizzle on us, or we will need a new spark to reignite it. Discussion is what separates the slow, tedious games from the games that are a blast.

Please read all of the rules so that I don't have to come up with more of these.

Explicitly starting RVS and asking for an RQS is in its motivation anti-town as well, because in a lot of games they aren’t really necessary anyways.

1.5 questions for most players here though, for convenience:
What do you want to be referred as (shorthand nickname), and what’s is your gender pronoun?[/quote]
I see someone has a bit of salt from playing Avalon. I do agree though that RVS/RQS is fairly pointless, and it's just much better to just start throwing votes around, even if you don't know what you're doing.

Anyways, I'm a he and you can refer to me as e. Hey look that rhymes!

post_rendered:
Okay since none of you seem willing to go read a game, I'll explain why that's a terrible idea.

There are 7 townies and 2 scum. If scum kills a townie before we hang someone, the game might go like this:

6:2 (NK)
5:2 (hang, 6/8)
4:2 (NK)
(At this point, hanging another townie loses the game, so it's better to let another player die to increase the chances of hanging scum.)
3:2 (NK)

That leaves a 3/4 chance of a 3:2 LyLo, and a 1/4 chance of reaching 5 players with less than two scum.

On the other hand, if we do hang today, it goes like this:

6:2 (hang, 7/9)
5:2 (NK)
4:2 (hang, 5/7)
3:2 (NK)

That leaves a 5/9 chance of a 3:2 LyLo, and a 4/9 > 1/4 chance of reaching five players with less than two scum.

This isn't even factoring in PR claims or the like. Also, if you're under the impression that town can't help find scum during daytime, you're simply in the wrong forum.

The nine of them drank their last drinks in silence, eyeing the others suspiciously.
“So. Who’s first?” one finally began.
A few accusations.

Hm. It’s nice of them to stick around. To find the killers, HannahW commented.
Mmmm, mathjay replied.

The two hovered invisibly in a corner. For the moment, at least, they had nowhere to go. Their bodies still laid in the back room.

“I can’t imagine how anyone could do such a thing!” someone exclaimed randomly.
All eyes turned and regarded the speaker apprehensively.
“I think he did it,” another declared, and several nodded.
“No, it’s not me.”

As the light faded, so did the discussion. Their drinks were long gone, and no one had yet had the nerve to go get some more.

post_rendered:
It was late. It was dark. It was ominous.

“Eight little boozies sleeping in their beds...”

It was around that time when it’s impossible to tell whether it was insanely late or diabolically early.

“One fell off and bumped his head...”

It was exactly 4:08 in the morning, and someone was whispering a nursery rhyme.

“The mama called the doctor and the doctor said, here’s a bullet BOOM now you’re dead! HAHA!”

“...Jesus Christ, say it louder why don’t you?”

“Shush. I know it didn’t rhyme but I felt so evil, so it’s worth it.”

“Don’t shush me. Is this the right room?”

“I think so.”

“Jesus. You think so?”

“Uh huh, now gimme the gun. I wanna shoot. Bam bam bam!”

“No, are you insane? We’re slitting his throat.”

“Ew, blood, are you insane? Pot, meet kettle.”

“I hate you.”

And so the heinous deed was committed.

aroodman was killed. He was a Sloppy Joe (Vanilla Townie).

post_rendered:
Oh my god, W H A T did I just read? This game is so Y I K E S.

mathjay, please quit being a bad mod and update the OP's alive, dead and significant events section. Thanks.

I understand that I replaced AUsername123 so I'm technically him but I still included an analysis on his day 1 actions just for completeness.

So here are my thoughts on everyone.

cai40
Confirmed town. Claimed 1-Shot BP with no CCs.

We either have a jailkeeper or a tracker then. I'm already getting 205 flashbacks, but unfortunately mathjay is a mod this time so he can't carry us.

NDMath
Basically confirmed town in my opinion. He has made several very helpful contributions and I agree with his a lot of reads. Most importantly, his motivations do not seem very scummy.

In post 321, he was the second person to push rf20008. This was before the BP claim and was going against majority opinion of the cai40 wagon at the time, so I do not think scum would want to out themselves like this.

Mafia knows that both rf20008 and cai40 were town. Hence, they would not have really bothered to discriminate between either of them. The hang priority on day 1 (before cai40 claimed) was cai40 then rf20008. Maintaining this status quo would have been beneficial for mafia; hence, drawing attention by hopping on the rf20008 counterwagon was completely unnecessary for mafia.

AUsername123
This is technically myself because I replaced in for AUsername123, however I will still use a speculative tone because I am describing my thought process as I read his day 1 posts.

Towards the end of day 1, he had less contributions, but at the beginning he was consistently making reads and trying to be helpful. Were all of them huge contributions? No. But the effort at least makes me think he isn't very likely to be mafia.

However, what makes me hesitate is the difference in tone between his first post
AUsername123 wrote:
boom badabing badaboom
i srsly hate early game cuz idk when does RVS ends and real stuff starts
For convenience just call me AU or auser and im a he

First thing first im having mixed feelings for #142 to #146 and #149. First of all cai is at 3 votes but then rf votes cai making it at 4 (luckily majo is off), later cai self votes making it at 5, now heres the thing after cai self votes rf just says "its twilight now" from this sentence we can conclude that rf doesnt know that majority is off and assumed cai is already hanged, but if you look at the tone i cant even imagine the tone as someone whos panicking because of a QL, looking calm even thus making me think that this is what rf wants but then 145 came with an even calmer tone than 144 but its cai who posted 145...like if its not semi closed id even say cai was jester tbh but i think from this we can assume that in 90% case cai is a vanilla (leaning to vt than mg) tho i think we can remove the possibility cai being a PR from that but idk. Thats enough about cai now about rf...its not even 1.5 real days after D1 starts and when rf thinks that a hang happened they still seemed calm, too calm to be town imo, last but not least i can somehow sense that on #149 rf seems disappointed? (maybe its a conf bias since idk what does that emote mean) so for now im on Vote: rf2008 unless theres someone even more sus

also is it just me or rf been tunneling cai nearly all the time or rf just being stupid

I cant exactly find anything juicy enough yet so thats for now ig

and his post 297
AUsername123 wrote:
ok so i honestly dont know on how to read others aside from cai/rf but eh if someone makes a good read then i can progress from there, for actual contrib tho it would take me at least D2 so that i get info from flips at least, thus for D1 i wont be doing much unless theres a read which im confident in and can actually add something

1 thing to note is that i dont really understand these latest interaction between arood/vain so eh halp?

It occurs to me as strange that such a huge shift in tone would occur. His first post has a confident tone, showing that he can use a good chain of logic to make a scumreads, but his second post is strangely unconfident and hesitant. This might be NAI but I'm just pointing it out.

Also, he was the first person to vote rf20008 at a time when the cai40 wagon was going strong so for the same reason I townread NDMath, I think that AUsername123 should be town as well.

Again, I replaced in for him so I know what I am but I just included this for completeness.

nikenissan
Very scummy because he's just taking other people’s opinions as his own.

If you just read through the thread, you'll see that he's "contributing" but what he's really doing is taking other people's posts and paraphrasing them into his own words. He sheeps and bandwagons a lot but tries to cover it up for some reason. I'm not gonna quote because it's literally 75% of his posts but if you go scroll through the thread, find a non-fluff post from him that talks about an actual read, and you scroll up a little further, I guarantee you that aroodman has said something very similar just a few posts before.

A more blatant example would be when he voted NDMath for no other reason than "aroodman told me so and he turned out to be VT." This by itself is suspicious, even more when NDMath is acting so towny. At best, this is bad town trying to follow advice from an SE. At worst, it is mafia trying to start a wagon to hang one of the most towny players in the game.

When he occasionally comes up with his own reads, you can tell the difference because they are very shallow compared to the analysis done by NDMath, aroodman, and AUsername123. However, this could be attributed to inexperience.

Furthermore, as evidenced in this post,
nikenissan wrote:
I’m not going to discuss more about the situation now

Anyways, what do you guys think a far on who to vote?

He's just running away from aroodman's questions because he doesn't have an answer that justifies a town motivation.

Finally, his LAMIST play is just bad. Generally, town would not care about how strong or weak they look because they always have a good defense for what they do – simply because they’re town. He seems to care way too much about how his actions look to others and that to me is a scumtell in and of itself. I also believe that you should defend yourself no matter how big or small the accusation is, even if your defense is just “Your accusation is complete BS, where’s your evidence?”

Overall, I think that nikenissan's behavior is pretty scummy.

mok
mok’s behavior is also scummy like nikenissan.

Similar to nikenissan, he bandwagons and sheeps other players (mostly aroodman). I’m not sure if that indicates inexperience or scum, but at least he doesn’t try to cover up his tracks, unlike nikenissan. At times, he even just goes
mok wrote:
Same @above

which is clearly not an attempt to hide the fact that he’s sheeping and not contributing.

He does come up with his own reads but they are also very shallow. Again, it could be attributed to inexperience.

His attempts to look “unbiased” is what makes me very suspicious. For example, he defends rf20008 by saying that he has played a few games before with rf20008 and his town meta is anti-town. Defending a player that looks so scummy is a strange move to me. I can’t see town doing this even off of a meta read, because rf20008 was literally just ignoring all accusations and just in general being extremely scummy. The scum motivation for defending rf20008 could be that he knew the rf20008 would flip town, so after rf20008 was hanged, he doesn’t look suspicious for pushing rf20008 and gains credibility for defending someone who turned out to be a VT. In reality, it just makes you even more suspicious because you seem to be burdened with TMI (too much information) which is a pretty big scumtell.

Overall, I think that mok is pretty suspicious.

Tie-Dye
I literally have no read on Tie-Dye due to his inactivity. He has a total of 8 posts in the thread, and no meaningful ones.

Hopefully his replacement VKU427 will be a bit more active, though VKU427 has yet to post anything in the thread.

Fvr_Vain
Fvr_Vain is a null.

Some of his reads I don’t agree with, but the subject’s statement accuracy is unimportant when it comes to scumreading. If anything, too much accuracy may indicate TMI and hence a scumtell. I think that his reads were pretty genuine and I couldn’t detect much scum motivation for scumreading cai40 and rf20008 and townreading nikenissan at that point in the game, they were both trolling and being generally scummy.

Fvr_Vain started being less active around EoD on day 1 so I don’t really have much on him after that.


Possible Scumteam
nikenissan and mok is a possible scumteam. They don’t really acknowledge each other’s existence much, and their few interactions were insignificant. mok only mentioned nikenissan’s name once and that was during the vote in RVS. nikenissan addressed mok a couple of times. To me, it’s unnatural that two semi-active players would avoid each other like that.

“This man is obviously guilty,” boomed Gentleman Number 0, waving around a hammer. “I will boink him with much expediency.”

“No, you fools, it is not!” intoned Gentleman Number 0, rifling through mok's pockets to find his identification papers. “It, is a thug, quite obviously, just let me see -- allow me a hot second right here -- I do believe I have... oh sh-”

Alright, so we got three potential scum, nikenissan, NDMath, and Fvr_Vain. Now the thing is, I don't see nikenissan and NDMath as being w/w because of their interactions, mainly this post:
nikenissan wrote:
Also, NDMath, I don’t see much about you. Why not share how you are any less scummier or towny. In fact, this whole game your chasing after me. Who knows, maybe your scum-team and trying to put it on me?

I doubt that this interaction is a WIFOM soft buss, especially when it comes from a newbie. But they cannot be v/v because there exists two mafia, so nikenissan and NDMath must be w/v. From this, we deduce that no matter who is the wolf amongst these two, Fvr_Vain has to be the second scum.

Let’s first go through nikenissan’s posts. On day 2, I have already gone through the fact that he is just sheeping people and generally not contributing to the discussion, so I’m not going to repeat what I have already said about that.

Throughout the game, nikenissan has been making a lot of double-edged sword comments. What I mean by this is that many of his posts say something like “He could be scum but could also be town.” This basically equates to saying nothing at all. An example would be posts 105 and 106:
nikenissan wrote:
cai40’s playing a villain role, which maybe is reverse psychology, so honestly I doubt he is the mafia
nikenissan wrote:
But maybe it’s not reverse psychology, he really is the villain in this scenario

As of now it’s speculation...what do you guys think?

These types of posts tend to come from mafia more than town for the reason that mafia is more likely to try to give themselves a way to back out of reads or claims that they make should something happen (for example, the BP claim from cai40) to confirm someone that they previously scumread. Townies don't tend to say stuff like this because they aren't consciously trying to avoid getting caught.

Furthermore, there is a lack of interactions between Fvr_Vain and nikenissan. The only few interactions I found while reading through the thread are when nikenissan asked a question like "What is hangbait?" and Fvr_Vain answers him. You can clearly see nikenissan is making an effort to distance himself from Fvr_Vain throughout the game. He has made almost no attempt to comment on Fvr_Vain's reads at all. If you start from post 502, you will see that during my argument with Fvr_Vain, nikenissan has made quite a few posts in between. But none of them even acknowledges Fvr_Vain's existence, instead, he's just firing shots at NDMath and me. This is just very strange behavior.

On the other hand, we have NDMath. He was defending mok early in day 2, so when I first replaced in, I thought he and mok were a possible scumteam. But it turns out that this is not the case, as we saw from the day 2 hang.

In my opinion, what clears NDMath the most are his interactions with Fvr_Vain. Fvr_Vain has questioned his reads quite a few times and I don't think it comes from w/w. For example, this post
Fvr_Vain wrote:
why the hell are we derpclearing mok?
@ND I’m looking at you

If we go off the assumption that Fvr_Vain is definitely scum for reasons explained above, this aggressive question would most likely not have come from a scumeteam. If Fvr_Vain was trying to buss NDMath, then he would have launched a direct attack on NDMath instead of breadcrumbing a few questions here and there that go completely overlooked. In any case, if Fvr_Vain was w/w with NDMath, I'd venture to guess that NDMath would be bussing Fvr_Vain, not the other way around - the less scummy person always busses the more scummy person. These subtle things make me think NDMath is definitely not w/w with Fvr_Vain.

So my scumteam theory is Fvr_Vain and nikenissan. What do you guys think?

I don't think it ever got explained well why cai40 was confirm town:

Hopefully Detailed Explanation
As this setup is a semi-open, we know all possible setups. For each setup if we list out just the town power roles these are the setups we have,

1. Jailkeeper, 1*BP
2. Jailkeeper
3. Cop, Doctor
4. Cop
5. Tracker, 1*BP
6. Tracker, Doctor
(#'s are arbitrary, just used for explanation.)

Because no one counterclaimed 1*BP or doctor we know we must be in setup 1, 2, 4, or 5.
Because both Socoobo and Vain claimed tracker, and no else claimed cop/jailkeeper we now know we must be in setup 5.
Setup 5 has a 1*BP, which then has to be cai40.
