post_rendered:
On the dawn of the day of doom, the dastardly dilemma darkened the delinquently delirious deliberations the daring deputation of drunkards were drowning in. The debate over the death of one of their own dominated the dramshop, droning on for the third day in a row. However the discussion was disrupted by many diverging dogmas, often derogatory, dragging the discourse into discord and directing some to dread the likely danger that the discharge of a fellow dipsomaniac would be desultory and doltishly dauntless.

None made this more clear than Gentleman Number 0.

“We find ourselves in a grave predicament,” he intoned in a grave voice.

“Wuzza... predishcate? Ew, grammar...” slurred Gentleman Number 1.

“Today, we have one last chance to finally the dastardly evil thugs who have committed such horrific acts of cold-blooded murder,” continued Gentleman Number 0, completely ignoring Gentleman Number 1. “We must bring justice to the poor victims. It is only right.”

“Mmm,” agreed Gentleman Number 2 absent-mindedly.

“And even if we fail, today,” said Gentleman Number 0 grandly, “we die glorious deaths in the name of justice and chivalry!”

“...” said Gentleman Number 4, grimacing. “Let us do our best not to die gloriously then, shall we?”

“Of course, of course!” boomed Gentleman Number 0. “And to this end, I suggest utilizing a clever little plan that is totally not a game. It is surely the best way to proceed.”

“And what is this supposed plan, may I ask?” said Gentleman Number 4.

“Mmhmm?” said Gentleman Number 3, finally looking up from where he was playing cat’s cradle in a corner. “What he said. I agree. I am contributing to this discussion. This is fun.”

“Well,” said Gentleman Number 0, rubbing his hands. “It is basically democracy, but with guns. With no further discussion at all, we should all shoot, on the count of three, whoever we think is the bad guy. We’re Americans; we’re good at telling who’s the good guy and who’s the bad guy. Easy peasy lemon squeezy!” explained Gentleman Number 0 enthusiastically. “Although, ‘one, two, three, shoot!’ sounds boring, so we should sing a nursery rhyme instead.”

“Me mamma used to sing me nurshery rhymes...” mumbled Gentleman Number 1.

“Exactly! I propose Ring Around the Rosie. Such a classic, I am quite fond of nursery rhymes, I must admit,” said Gentleman Number 0, rubbing his chin. “Oh, one more thing. Make sure to shoot people in the legs or like the stomach so they don’t die, at least not immediately. Ah, democracy is a wonderful thing. It never goes wrong, does it?”

“...” said Gentleman Number 4. “Oh-kay. Frankly speaking, sir, anyone with a brain would be able to tell this is a horrible, stupidious, duplicitously dumb idea. However, I LIKE IT. I like it very much. WE SHALL PLAY YOUR GAME.”

And so they did.

“Ring around the rosie...”

The last remaining five of the bold, loyal patrons of Where it’s Less Crowded linked hands and began to stumble around in a circle.

“Pocket full of posies...”

Two murderers, three innocents, at the mercy of a game.

“Ashes...”

A gunshot.

“Ashes...”

A second gunshot.

“And we all fall down,” finished Gentleman Number 0 with an evil grin. “Bang.”


nikenissan was hanged. He was a Sloppy Joe (Vanilla Townie).

The mafia faction has achieved parity with town. As a result, the game is now over.

Congratulations to e_is_cool, Fvr_Vain, and NDMath for winning the game. The Mafia team has won.

Transparency Report
A transparency report consisting of moderator decisions or actions taken during the game that may have impacted gameplay in a negative way have been compiled for player perusal. The severity of the incidents detailed here, and how much they impacted gameplay, is somewhat debatable. Internally, we have determined that moderator actions did not majorly impact gameplay, or the outcome of the game, which we are thankful for. However, we do apologize for the negligence and the disorganization that led to these incidents. All relevant events have been disclosed in this report, for the sake of player-moderator trust and transparency. Please contact the moderators via a Private Message if you have further questions or comments.

1. Accidentally informed Fvr_Vain he was town instead of scum.

After the roles were randed but before role PM's were sent out, mathjay informed Fvr_Vain via Discord that he was town. He immediately corrected himself, and informed Fvr_Vain that he was scum and that his partner was e_is_cool. This most likely did not affect game balance at all, the only issue being that Fvr_Vain learned of his alignment roughly a day before official role PMs were sent out.

2. Accidentally sent a VT role pm to AUSername123, who replaced TheMagician (fixed before he saw it).

HannahW accidentally sent a VT role PM to AUsername123 upon him replacing into the game (Post #189). She caught it right away and fixed it within three minutes. According to AoPS, AUsername123 was not online at that time, and there were no other people viewing the PM.

3. Potential modspew incident in Day 3.

On August 11, 2020, on the first day of Day 3, Socoobo responded to a question regarding his night results. As the tracker, he had received a “No result.” This was an error made on the part of the mods, as the correct result should have been “no movement” or “your target did not visit anyone.” However, when asked to clarify, Socoobo quoted the mods in an attempt to verify his tracker claim:
Socoobo wrote:
I got "No result." from HannahW. Those were the exact words.
At the same time, mathjay posted the following:
mathjay wrote:
NDMath wrote:
Did you get 'No result' or 'did not visit' since those are two different things?
this is an irrelevant question as VKU427 has flipped as a VT and therefore does not have any night actions, and the tracker role cannot ever be roleblocked. Therefore the distinction was not made.
mathjay was distracted at the time of this post and had not seen Socoobo’s post. He was responding to NDMath’s question directly in an attempt to shut down that avenue of questioning, regardless of whether Socoobo was the real tracker or not. This post, however, was shortsighted and could have easily been interpreted as modspew, confirming Socoobo to be the real tracker. The correct response should have been to not say anything, or change the wording to say “the distinction would not have been made.”

In response, the moderators shut down the thread while discussing how to resolve the situation. After consulting with the mafia team, the chosen solution was to lock votes onto the other thunderdome, so the modspew issue concerning tracker claims would not affect the LYLO hang. The officially given reasoning was that the rules had been changed to locked votes at LYLO, and the other thunderdome (nikenissan/NDMath) was chosen because it required the least amount of changes to the current votes.

This was a serious incident that could have affected gameplay. Major errors were made by the moderators, including the unclear tracker result and the shortsighted intervention, and we apologize for that. We will do our best to ensure this will never happen again. Thankfully, however, we believe this incident has not affected the outcome of this particular game.


General Feedback
Comments on Day 1
By mathjay

It’s my personal opinion that what e_is_cool and Fvr_Vain said regarding RVS and RQS is a bunch of hot garbage. RVS votes in and of themselves are not alignment indicative, and different players prefer different levels of engagement in non-serious RVS (this is not alignment indicative either). Calling extended RVS antitown is also arguably a stretch; it only becomes antitown when it actively interferes with serious discussion.

A bit of commentary on cai40’s early claims to be mafia. This is not something you should normally do because town will immediately start talking about it, eating up discussion time that could be used for other, more useful things. I could see an argument for doing this if you are a PR (power role) such as a cop or a doctor in order to avoid being the mafia factional kill (NK), but cai40 was a 1-shot BP, and a BP’s worth resides in drawing a NK and wasting it by not dying. Consequently, this is counterproductive to both a standard town win condition (wincon) and cai40’s specific PR wincon.

In hindsight, Fvr_Vain’s reads in #162 were very bad and he should have been called out more aggressively for it. He shamelessly scumread trolling and implied a scumread on aroodman by saying “You have generally tried to put attention on others” and “motivation here seem to be an attempt to get self out of hang pool,” both of which are poor, speculative attacks and should have been scumread coming from a player of his experience level.

cai40, mok, and rf20008 in general did not respond well to efforts by aroodman to prod them into contributing or playing the game, which was unfortunate. This shameless apathy is detrimental to town (and is arguably one of the most significant contributors to the town loss) because it a) is something scum can exploit to lurk unnoticed, b) is something scum can (and did) exploit to push mishangs, c) interferes with town’s ability to form accurate reads, and d) means that town were essentially missing several of their members.

Also in hindsight, Fvr_Vain saying that “theres a difference between bad play and anti-town play. bad play can still be pro town” really should have raised more eyebrows, even though it’s arguably not alignment indicative in isolation.

There were no major issues surrounding cai40’s PR claim. As the top wagon 2 days before EOD, it was correct for them to claim there. It was also correct to automatically believe cai40’s claim, barring any counterclaims by the real BP or other setup-incompatible roles.

Comments on Day 2
By mathjay

Day 2 was honestly fine. No major issues to comment on.

Comments on Day 3
By mathjay

Started out well. Reminder by Socoobo about how a single incorrect vote in LYLO could lose the game was very nice. Tracker claim was also correct: LYLO is where town throws in everything including the kitchen sink. All options are on the table, because it is literally win-or-lose by the EOD vote.

The tracker counterclaim from scum was also mechanically correct.

Two thunderdomes at EOD is not something that you should do if votes are locked and deadline hang is off, because that would result in an auto-win for mafia because the day would end in a no-hang. In this case, however, since votes weren’t locked at the start of LYLO, it was nonstandard but not game-ending.

As a reminder, you should never be able to verify any claims through any variation of “the mod said so.” Moderator errors were involved in this incident, and for that, I apologize (more details can be found in the transparency report). But please do not quote moderator communications or rely on the moderator to try to verify your claim in the future.

Also, this is another reminder to never hang with a significant margin left before EOD as town. Even if it feels like there’s nothing more to do, it’s always smart to wait, because things can change. Maybe in the roughly 13 days that were left, nikenissan could have managed to convince people that he was town. We’ll never know, but that was a possibility.


Most of the problems from this game stemmed from a significant number of players being inactive, not participating in scumhunting or discussions, and generally playing extremely suboptimally. Throughout the game, despite attempts from the SEs to prod discussion, most of their efforts were not very successful. We unfortunately cannot think of an easy way to fix this. However, we would like to bring your attention back to the three basic things you need to do as town:

1. Be active. Discuss things. Question things.
2. Be towny. Can be done by doing above.
3. Find scum. We know it’s not as simple as that, but some more details are scattered throughout feedback.

If you would like to know what specifically you could do better in future games, we recommend checking out your personal feedback tab. If you have more questions, you can ask us in your role PM. Apologies for how long this took.

First and foremost, please, please, please do not post in a game if you are not in it.
You were quite active and posted a lot, which is really good. However, as many pointed out, your posts contained very little content. It seems that a large part of this is because you’re not sure what to do or how to make reads.
When this is the case, I always advise asking questions. You can ask questions to others. They can be questions on how to play if you’re confused: like, what sorts of things are generally scummy and why (see general feedback for the answer to this)? They can also be clarification and information questions: why did you say this or that, how did you come to this conclusion, what is your read on person x and why, how do you read person x’s action a, who do you think is scummier out of persons x and y and why, etc.
You can also ask questions as you read: does this come from scum or town or both, what kind of things will town do, what kind of things will the mafia -- whose goal is to kill and frame the townies -- do, why to the answers to all of those questions, and why and how to any thought or read you may have.
You asked questions a few times in the game, which was good, but you didn’t follow up on those questions.
Once you’ve received the answers to your questions, don’t just leave them there. Think about it and ask follow-up questions or ask yourself how this impacts your reads (who is town and who is scum according to you).
Overall, just ask questions if you don’t know what’s going on. Ask specific questions instead of vague ones if possible, and things can be worked out from there.

I think something you have to understand here is that contribution is town-positive, and there is no way around that. If you are being scumread for a lack of contribution, you can fix it relatively easily: keep playing the game, keep trying to develop your ability to come up with reads, and try to be proactive. Good reads are essential to good mafia play - both townplay and scumplay - so by developing them, you will get better at mafia while also being (hopefully) more townread. If you need help with this, many people, myself included, have written posts explaining basic how-to’s; they can be found in the Resources section, or near the beginnings of recent newbie games. Alternatively, you could just ask any SEs or ICs in any future games you play in.

One basic, basic tip to get you started on developing better reads: always ask questions. Someone does something? Immediately ask yourself: is that better for town, or better for scum, or neither? Or are you not sure? Ask these questions to yourself, and if you have trouble figuring it out or aren’t sure if you reached the “right” conclusion, you can and should ask other people for their opinions.

I think at this point, you have a pretty solid grasp on gameplay and fundamentals, as well as being able to tell whether something is a somewhat stupid idea or not. The next step, here, is being more proactive. There are multiple ways to do this - one of them being more reads, of course - but one thing you can work on is pressure. For instance, is someone lurking? Is someone posting questionable stuff, messing up your reads on them? Then something you can do here is actively go after them. Ask them questions. Ask them about their thoughts, their reads. Ask them to stop trolling. Vote them. Go through their posts, look for common scumtells, and interrogate them over why they did those things.

If you do this, one of three things will happen: 1, you will gain a better understanding of their thought process, developing a more accurate read on them even if their posts look questionable at first glance; 2, they will buckle under the pressure you are putting them under, potentially freezing up and becoming even more scummy; or, 3, they will actually start paying attention to the game, contributing, and being active.

All of these three things are amazing for town and can turn around a game, so applying pressure is definitely something you can think about in your future games. Of course, you have to make sure you aren’t overly tunneling or confirmation-biasing, and you should stop to reconsider if you feel like you’ve gone too deep. And if you end up pressuring a PR, there’s a chance that won’t end up well either. However, on the whole, being proactive is definitely better than watching people mess up and troll and just doom the game.

Best of luck in your future games!

9.3 Socoobo
From mathjay:

Hi, you did a decent job this game, so I’ll be going in detail into some of the things you can keep in mind in the future.

First of all, I think you should be more careful when handing out townreads. Your motivation reads are fine, but also in hindsight, a lot of them were wrong. Like, for example, let’s take your reads in Post #452.

One important thing to consider when making reads is context. If the person you're reading is an IC, hold them to IC standards. Be more cautious in giving them towncred. ND in this case didn’t really need to do anything because neither he or Fvr_Vain were being pressured. He could win without doing anything, as long as the status quo stayed the same. So, just because someone doesn’t seem to have scum motivation does not mean that they’re not scum.

On the other hand, if the person you're reading is more new, be more cautious in reading what they do as scummy. TWTBAW is very bad, and it feels horrible to use, but in newbie games especially, I think it’s a necessity. Because when you make reads without TWTBAW, just straight, textbook motivation reads, you are making reads under the assumption that town will play optimally. You cannot make this assumption in newbie games.

Also, you should always be ready to reconsider reads. Unless you’re confident that someone you’re townreading cannot possibly be that towny as scum, you should always be ready to reconsider your read in the back of your mind. Not sure if this was an issue this game, but it’s still something to keep in mind.

Honestly the biggest issue you ran into this game was the issue of TWTBAW. As I said above, it’s yucky but really unavoidable in newbie games, and even games that are not newbie games. Even in the invitational, people had to use TWTBAW. My best advice is to cut everything to the very, very bare basics, and to be stingy with both townreads and scumreads. Try to prod people and ask them questions until you get a better grasp of their thought process, and also pressure them if you can. Don’t let people lurk for free. Also try to look at interactions if you can, and be very, very aware of the trap of reading based off of competency. In a lot of games but newbie games especially, better play does NOT equal town.

Another more specific critique with something that turned out to be pretty significant:
Socoobo wrote:
nikenissan wrote:
Avoiding Game Prod

Anyways, I don't understand how sheeping makes me any more scummier. What makes mafia more likely to sheep than town?

Most mafia use this strategy to make it seem like they’re contributing to the discussion while they are really not. By doing this, you’re just repeating ideas that have already been said and that’s really anti-town. Not only does it not contribute to the discussion but it also clogs up the thread.
It’s critical to understand here that bandwagoning, sheeping, and even lurking - in short, most/all wikitells - are not specifically something that scum WANT to do. Will they bandwagon and sheep if they can get away with it? Yeah. But bandwagoning and sheeping doesn’t have scum motivation specifically, and scum - especially good scum - won’t intentionally do it, if that makes sense. I mean, why would scum do it when they would get scumread? It’s usually not worth it.

So be careful when you’re scumreading people for stuff like that. Because it’s definitely bad for town, and town shouldn’t do it. That doesn’t mean scum want to do it either, cuz it’ll get them scumread and that’s bad for them too. This also ties into the TWTBAW concepts we were talking about earlier.

On Day 3, your analysis on nikenissan was decent. But in your Fvr_Vain analysis, you assumed optimal scum play. Like with optimal town play, that is NOT an assumption you should be making. Honestly, that’s the trickiest part of motivation reads. Just try to be aware of what assumptions you are making. What would have to be true for this read to be true? Or more specifically, what would invalidate this read? What are its weaknesses? How can it be wrong? That way, if you’re aware of how your reads can go sideways and what specifically they are based on, you’ll immediately recognize it when you see something that tells you your reads might be wrong and you have to reconsider.
Socoobo wrote:
These subtle things make me think NDMath is definitely not w/w with Fvr_Vain.
I would honestly hesitate before you use subtle tells in “definite” reads. I don’t know whether these were your actual reads, or whether you privately were more reserved, but it’s something to think about. Granted, it was LYLO where you don’t have much other choice, but still.

All of this is concerning motivation reads, which are honestly really hard to get right. There’s not really much else I can say about them, except that better reads will come with experience. You did decently well this game, and you put in decent effort. You also seem to have a good grasp on how the game works, general gameplay, your wincons, and most common mistakes, although voting early in LYLO was unfortunate. Still, good job. It was a nice try. Better luck in your future games, and I hope this helped!

[hide=Transparency Report]
A transparency report consisting of moderator decisions or actions taken during the game that may have impacted gameplay in a negative way have been compiled for player perusal. The severity of the incidents detailed here, and how much they impacted gameplay, is somewhat debatable. Internally, we have determined that moderator actions did not majorly impact gameplay, or the outcome of the game, which we are thankful for. However, we do apologize for the negligence and the disorganization that led to these incidents. All relevant events have been disclosed in this report, for the sake of player-moderator trust and transparency. Please contact the moderators via a Private Message if you have further questions or comments. 

[b]1. Accidentally informed Fvr_Vain he was town instead of scum.[/b]

After the roles were randed but before role PM's were sent out, mathjay informed Fvr_Vain via Discord that he was town. He immediately corrected himself, and informed Fvr_Vain that he was scum and that his partner was e_is_cool. This most likely did not affect game balance at all, the only issue being that Fvr_Vain learned of his alignment roughly a day before official role PMs were sent out. 

[b]2. Accidentally sent a VT role pm to AUSername123, who replaced TheMagician (fixed before he saw it).[/b]

HannahW accidentally sent a VT role PM to AUsername123 upon him replacing into the game ([url=https://aops.com/community/p16382547]Post #189[/url]). She caught it right away and fixed it within three minutes. According to AoPS, AUsername123 was not online at that time, and there were no other people viewing the PM.

[b]3. Potential modspew incident in Day 3.[/b]

On August 11, 2020, on the first day of Day 3, Socoobo responded to a question regarding his night results. As the tracker, he had received a “No result.” This was an error made on the part of the mods, as the correct result should have been “no movement” or “your target did not visit anyone.” However, when asked to clarify, Socoobo quoted the mods in an attempt to verify his tracker claim: 
[quote name="Socoobo" url="/community/p17042950"]
I got "No result." from HannahW. Those were the exact words.
[/quote]
At the same time, mathjay posted the following: 
[quote name="mathjay" url="/community/p17043027"]
[quote name="NDMath" url="/community/p17042937"]
Did you get 'No result' or 'did not visit' since those are two different things?
[/quote]
[color=#4A86E8]this is an irrelevant question as VKU427 has flipped as a VT and therefore does not have any night actions, and the tracker role cannot ever be roleblocked. Therefore the distinction was not made. [/color]
[/quote]
mathjay was distracted at the time of this post and had not seen Socoobo’s post. He was responding to NDMath’s question directly in an attempt to shut down that avenue of questioning, regardless of whether Socoobo was the real tracker or not. This post, however, was shortsighted and could have easily been interpreted as modspew, confirming Socoobo to be the real tracker. The correct response should have been to not say anything, or change the wording to say “the distinction would not have been made.” 

In response, the moderators shut down the thread while discussing how to resolve the situation. After consulting with the mafia team, the chosen solution was to lock votes onto the other thunderdome, so the modspew issue concerning tracker claims would not affect the LYLO hang. The officially given reasoning was that the rules had been changed to locked votes at LYLO, and the other thunderdome (nikenissan/NDMath) was chosen because it required the least amount of changes to the current votes. 

This was a serious incident that could have affected gameplay. Major errors were made by the moderators, including the unclear tracker result and the shortsighted intervention, and we apologize for that. We will do our best to ensure this will never happen again. Thankfully, however, we believe this incident has not affected the outcome of this particular game. 
[/hide]

[hide=General Feedback]
[hide=Comments on Day 1]
[b]By mathjay[/b]

It’s my personal opinion that what e_is_cool and Fvr_Vain said regarding RVS and RQS is a bunch of hot garbage. RVS votes in and of themselves are not alignment indicative, and different players prefer different levels of engagement in non-serious RVS (this is not alignment indicative either). Calling extended RVS antitown is also arguably a stretch; it only becomes antitown when it actively interferes with serious discussion. 

A bit of commentary on cai40’s early claims to be mafia. This is not something you should normally do because town will immediately start talking about it, eating up discussion time that could be used for other, more useful things. I could see an argument for doing this if you are a PR (power role) such as a cop or a doctor in order to avoid being the mafia factional kill (NK), but cai40 was a 1-shot BP, and a BP’s worth resides in drawing a NK and wasting it by not dying. Consequently, this is counterproductive to both a standard town win condition (wincon) and cai40’s specific PR wincon. 

In hindsight, Fvr_Vain’s reads in [url=https://aops.com/community/p16346140]#162[/url] were very bad and he should have been called out more aggressively for it. He shamelessly scumread trolling and implied a scumread on aroodman by saying “You have generally tried to put attention on others” and “motivation here seem to be an attempt to get self out of hang pool,” both of which are poor, speculative attacks and should have been scumread coming from a player of his experience level. 

cai40, mok, and rf20008 in general did not respond well to efforts by aroodman to prod them into contributing or playing the game, which was unfortunate. This shameless apathy is detrimental to town (and is  arguably one of the most significant contributors to the town loss) because it a) is something scum can exploit to lurk unnoticed, b) is something scum can (and did) exploit to push mishangs, c) interferes with town’s ability to form accurate reads, and d) means that town were essentially missing several of their members. 

Also in hindsight, Fvr_Vain saying that “theres a difference between bad play and anti-town play. bad play can still be pro town” really should have raised more eyebrows, even though it’s arguably not alignment indicative in isolation. 

There were no major issues surrounding cai40’s PR claim. As the top wagon 2 days before EOD, it was correct for them to claim there. It was also correct to automatically believe cai40’s claim, barring any counterclaims by the real BP or other setup-incompatible roles. 
[/hide]
[hide=Comments on Day 2]
[b]By mathjay[/b]

Day 2 was honestly fine. No major issues to comment on. 
[/hide]
[hide=Comments on Day 3]
[b]By mathjay[/b]

Started out well. Reminder by Socoobo about how a single incorrect vote in LYLO could lose the game was very nice. Tracker claim was also correct: LYLO is where town throws in everything including the kitchen sink. All options are on the table, because it is literally win-or-lose by the EOD vote. 

The tracker counterclaim from scum was also mechanically correct. 

Two thunderdomes at EOD is not something that you should do if votes are locked and deadline hang is off, because that would result in an auto-win for mafia because the day would end in a no-hang. In this case, however, since votes weren’t locked at the start of LYLO, it was nonstandard but not game-ending. 

As a reminder, you should never be able to verify any claims through any variation of “the mod said so.” Moderator errors were involved in this incident, and for that, I apologize (more details can be found in the transparency report). But please do not quote moderator communications or rely on the moderator to try to verify your claim in the future. 

Also, this is another reminder to never hang with a significant margin left before EOD as town. Even if it feels like there’s nothing more to do, it’s always smart to wait, because things can change. Maybe in the roughly 13 days that were left, nikenissan could have managed to convince people that he was town. We’ll never know, but that was a possibility. 
[/hide]

Most of the problems from this game stemmed from a significant number of players being inactive, not participating in scumhunting or discussions, and generally playing extremely suboptimally. Throughout the game, despite attempts from the SEs to prod discussion, most of their efforts were not very successful. We unfortunately cannot think of an easy way to fix this. However, we would like to bring your attention back to the three basic things you need to do as town:

1. Be active. Discuss things. Question things. 
2. Be towny. Can be done by doing above.
3. Find scum. We know it’s not as simple as that, but some more details are scattered throughout feedback.

If you would like to know what specifically you could do better in future games, we recommend checking out your personal feedback tab. If you have more questions, you can ask us in your role PM. Apologies for how long this took. 
[/hide]

[b]From HannahW:[/b]

First and foremost, please, please, please [u]do not post in a game if you are not in it.[/u]
You were quite active and posted a lot, which is really good. However, as many pointed out, your posts contained very little content. It seems that a large part of this is because you’re not sure what to do or how to make reads. 
When this is the case, I always advise asking questions. You can ask questions to others. They can be questions on how to play if you’re confused: like, what sorts of things are generally scummy and why (see general feedback for the answer to this)? They can also be clarification and information questions: why did you say this or that, how did you come to this conclusion, what is your read on person x and why, how do you read person x’s action a, who do you think is scummier out of persons x and y and why, etc. 
You can also ask questions as you read: does this come from scum or town or both, what kind of things will town do, what kind of things will the mafia -- whose goal is to kill and frame the townies -- do, why to the answers to all of those questions, and why and how to any thought or read you may have.
You asked questions a few times in the game, which was good, but you didn’t follow up on those questions. 
Once you’ve received the answers to your questions, don’t just leave them there. Think about it and ask follow-up questions or ask yourself how this impacts your reads (who is town and who is scum according to you).
Overall, just ask questions if you don’t know what’s going on. Ask specific questions instead of vague ones if possible, and things can be worked out from there.

It’s not entirely invalid reasoning, but it’s also pretty shaky. If you ever wanna learn more about this, you should ask people about TWTBAW (Too Wolfy to Be a Wolf), which is kinda a tricky subject, but it can help a lot in your scumreads. 

[quote name=mok url=https://aops.com/community/p16945013]
Uh didn’t aroodman say you guys are sus?[/quote]
Listening to what an experienced player says/has said is perfectly fine. However, there were two things you have to keep in mind while doing so: 1, keep in mind that player could be scum. aroodman flipped town here, so you’re fine on this count. And, 2, rather than blindly repeating what they say, it is always, always so much better for you to understand why they said what they said. In this case, the reason aroodman scumread NDMath was his apathy and dire lack of contribution as an IC. Being aware of this argument, and using it instead of saying “aroodman said so” could go a long way towards improving both your understanding of the game, and convincing other people that you’re right. 

Generally, you seemed to have a good grasp of what was happening in this game. However, I noticed that you didn’t really talk about the reasonings behind your reads when you posted them. Sometimes, that’s fine, but keep in mind that that can get you scumread. You’ll also do a better job convincing other people that you’re right if you give reasonings. Mafia is a game of dialogue after all, so remember to talk to people and respond to them when you answer questions. It’ll both help you make better reads, and other people will be able to make better reads on you too, so it’s a win-win! Best of luck in your future games! 

[b]From HannahW:[/b]

This is kind of what mathjay said, but rephrased and from a different angle: the reason you all weren’t able to pin NDMath as obvious scum after aroodman died was because you guys didn’t understand why ND was scummy in the first place.
The fact that this even happened -- everyone sheeping aroodman although no one knew what he was talking about -- is extremely problematic. 
Never ever vote anyone just because someone told you to or because everyone else is doing so. Realize that consensus does not equal your own thoughts. It’s easy to assume that if everyone else thinks x is scum, x is scum, using that as your only reason. However, if you cannot justify for yourself independently why x is scum other than that everyone else thinks the things x did were scummy… Just be very careful to differentiate the majority opinion from your own: if absolutely no one thought that what x did was scummy, would I still think that it’s scummy? Make sure to explore all sides of the issue on your own before joining the crowd.[/hide]

[b]From mathjay:[/b]

Congrats on your first game. From rereading the game, you were one of the most active people in the game, and you seemed to be on top of what was happening. You also seem to have a good grasp of how this game works, so we’ll be going over some of the detailed things you said. 

From Day 1, something interesting you said: 
[quote name=nikenissan url=https://aops.com/community/p16335806]
Defending myself makes me looks weak

Until you have a definite and well-structured attack am I going to do something about it

But I'm not letting my guard down over the simple words of: "ah les hang niken after"

If you want to vote for me without reason, so do it[/quote]
I believe aroodman commented on this, but defending yourself against attacks does not make you look weak. To the contrary, by providing a solid defense, you get a chance to explain your thoughts to other people and help people read you better. And even if it does make you look weak, it doesn’t matter because looking weak isn’t something most people care about in a game of mafia, as it doesn’t have much to do with towniness and scumminess. TL;DR, in most cases, you should defend yourself from attacks. If they’re baseless attacks, just call them baseless and move on. If not, refute them or at least acknowledge them. To most people, ignoring attacks doesn’t look very good. 
[quote name=nikenissan url=https://aops.com/community/p16336181]
A general strategy is to pick on the weak, not the strongest

So if I’m defending myself in such petty attacks, I look vulnerable and weak and thus more people will start unionizing or pick on me[/quote]
On top of what aroodman said, a) good town will not make “such petty attacks,” and b) in most cases, other people will recognize when people are making bad attacks on you, and it will end up looking worse for the person attacking you. 

[quote name=nikenissan url=https://aops.com/community/p16902000]
I don’t have unique reads; when I see another player’s reads that I agree with and/or similar to my thoughts, I proceed.

Now, I don’t understand how Fvr_Vain pointing out that NDMath (and Scoobdoo) puts me on scum-team for my lack of contributions.[/quote]
I think something you have to understand here is that contribution is town-positive, and there is no way around that. If you are being scumread for a lack of contribution, you can fix it relatively easily: keep playing the game, keep trying to develop your ability to come up with reads, and try to be proactive. Good reads are essential to good mafia play - both townplay and scumplay - so by developing them, you will get better at mafia while also being (hopefully) more townread. If you need help with this, many people, myself included, have written posts explaining basic how-to’s; they can be found in the Resources section, or near the beginnings of recent newbie games. Alternatively, you could just ask any SEs or ICs in any future games you play in. 

One basic, basic tip to get you started on developing better reads: always ask questions. Someone does something? Immediately ask yourself: is that better for town, or better for scum, or neither? Or are you not sure? Ask these questions to yourself, and if you have trouble figuring it out or aren’t sure if you reached the “right” conclusion, you can and should ask other people for their opinions. 

[quote name=nikenissan url=https://aops.com/community/p16965387]
At this point, I am not sure how to defend myself.[/quote]
If you are not sure how you can defend yourself from attacks, which is something that can happen sometimes, it’s helpful to move on to the next best thing: which, in this case, is contribution. And contribution doesn’t even have to be hard. If you’ve noticed, NDMath had been consistently asking you questions about your posts ([url=https://aops.com/community/p16766197]#439[/url], [url=https://aops.com/community/p16967756]#554[/url]). I believe you completely missed or ignored those posts, but NDMath was presumably genuinely trying to help you develop your reads. Answering those posts counts as contribution because you get to clarify your reads, and NDMath also would develop a better read on you (assuming he wasn’t scum as he was in this game). 

Now, I’m gonna quickly point out some problems with your Day 3 LYLO play that you should keep in mind in the future. 
[quote name=nikenissan url=https://aops.com/community/p17038149]
Also, why not show you are more town then us right now?[/quote]
At this point, you were in the scum pool POE. If you are still in POE at LYLO, your focus (as town) should be on trying to be as towny as possible and convince people that you are town. It doesn’t matter even if they’re reading you for bad reasons, because if they scumread you, you lose. So try your absolute best to be towny and win the POE fight. That is your wincon, and what you should have been thinking about at that moment. 
[quote name=nikenissan url=https://aops.com/community/p17038389]
I do not know how to defend myself, despite me being a Vanilla Townie, but that's definitely not good evidence to prevent me from getting hanged

So, I NEED to provide good contribution and not sheep ...[/quote]
You are correct here, and it’s very good that you realized this. Although the resulting contribution was not the best, that’s something you can work on and something that’ll improve with time. 
[quote name=nikenissan url=https://aops.com/community/p17039132]
Now, cai40 is out-of-the-picture. However, even though Scoobdoo claims he is Tracker, couldn’t we contest that?[/quote]
Not sure what you mean by contest, but unless someone else comes forward and claims tracker, Socoobo is always the real tracker, especially since this is LYLO. It would be gamethrowing for town to lie here. As a general rule in most setups, you should assume PR claims are real until there’s a reason you shouldn’t. 

So, overall, you did decently. It’s always tough being scumread, but you managed to power through it, even though you ultimately ended up being hanged. Better reads will come with more experience, but remember to always ask questions. Reads are also hard to definitively “prove;” all you can do is convince other people that you are right. Read through people’s posts, think about what stood out to you and what felt scummy, think about the town or scum motivations that could be possible, and then tell people your thoughts. Dialogue is important in mafia, so remember to talk to people. It’ll help you get a better read on them, and they’ll get a better read on you too. Best of luck in your future games!

[quote name="VKU427" url="/community/p16934202"]
this game...
THIS GAMEMEMMEMEMEM

this game is so trash

I think at this point, you have a pretty solid grasp on gameplay and fundamentals, as well as being able to tell whether something is a somewhat stupid idea or not. The next step, here, is being more proactive. There are multiple ways to do this - one of them being more reads, of course - but one thing you can work on is pressure. For instance, is someone lurking? Is someone posting questionable stuff, messing up your reads on them? Then something you can do here is actively go after them. Ask them questions. Ask them about their thoughts, their reads. Ask them to stop trolling. Vote them. Go through their posts, look for common scumtells, and interrogate them over why they did those things. 

If you do this, one of three things will happen: 1, you will gain a better understanding of their thought process, developing a more accurate read on them even if their posts look questionable at first glance; 2, they will buckle under the pressure you are putting them under, potentially freezing up and becoming even more scummy; or, 3, they will actually start paying attention to the game, contributing, and being active. 

All of these three things are amazing for town and can turn around a game, so applying pressure is definitely something you can think about in your future games. Of course, you have to make sure you aren’t overly tunneling or confirmation-biasing, and you should stop to reconsider if you feel like you’ve gone too deep. And if you end up pressuring a PR, there’s a chance that won’t end up well either. However, on the whole, being proactive is definitely better than watching people mess up and troll and just doom the game. 

Best of luck in your future games!

[hide=9.3 Socoobo]
[b]From mathjay:[/b]

Hi, you did a decent job this game, so I’ll be going in detail into some of the things you can keep in mind in the future. 

First of all, I think you should be more careful when handing out townreads. Your motivation reads are fine, but also in hindsight, a lot of them were wrong. Like, for example, let’s take your reads in [url=https://aops.com/community/p16808967]Post #452[/url]. 

One important thing to consider when making reads is context. If the person you're reading is an IC, hold them to IC standards. Be more cautious in giving them towncred. ND in this case didn’t really need to do anything because neither he or Fvr_Vain were being pressured. He could win without doing anything, as long as the status quo stayed the same. So, just because someone doesn’t seem to have scum motivation does not mean that they’re not scum. 

On the other hand, if the person you're reading is more new, be more cautious in reading what they do as scummy. TWTBAW is very bad, and it feels horrible to use, but in newbie games especially, I think it’s a necessity. Because when you make reads without TWTBAW, just straight, textbook motivation reads, you are making reads under the assumption that town will play optimally. You [i]cannot[/i] make this assumption in newbie games. 

Also, you should always be ready to reconsider reads. Unless you’re confident that someone you’re townreading cannot possibly be that towny as scum, you should always be ready to reconsider your read in the back of your mind. Not sure if this was an issue this game, but it’s still something to keep in mind. 

Honestly the biggest issue you ran into this game was the issue of TWTBAW. As I said above, it’s yucky but really unavoidable in newbie games, and even games that are not newbie games. Even in the invitational, people had to use TWTBAW. My best advice is to cut everything to the very, very bare basics, and to be stingy with both townreads and scumreads. Try to prod people and ask them questions until you get a better grasp of their thought process, and also pressure them if you can. Don’t let people lurk for free. Also try to look at interactions if you can, and be very, very aware of the trap of reading based off of competency. In a lot of games but newbie games especially, better play does NOT equal town. 

Another more specific critique with something that turned out to be pretty significant:
[quote name="Socoobo" url="/community/p16867430"]
[quote=nikenissan]Avoiding Game Prod

Anyways, I don't understand how sheeping makes me any more scummier. What makes mafia more likely to sheep than town?[/quote]

Most mafia use this strategy to make it seem like they’re contributing to the discussion while they are really not. By doing this, you’re just repeating ideas that have already been said and that’s really anti-town. Not only does it not contribute to the discussion but it also clogs up the thread.
[/quote]
It’s critical to understand here that bandwagoning, sheeping, and even lurking - in short, most/all wikitells - are not specifically something that scum WANT to do. Will they bandwagon and sheep if they can get away with it? Yeah. But bandwagoning and sheeping doesn’t have scum motivation specifically, and scum - especially good scum - won’t intentionally do it, if that makes sense. I mean, why would scum do it when they would get scumread? It’s usually not worth it. 

So be careful when you’re scumreading people for stuff like that. Because it’s definitely bad for town, and town shouldn’t do it. That doesn’t mean scum want to do it either, cuz it’ll get them scumread and that’s bad for them too. This also ties into the TWTBAW concepts we were talking about earlier. 

On Day 3, your analysis on nikenissan was decent. But in your Fvr_Vain analysis, you assumed optimal scum play. Like with optimal town play, that is NOT an assumption you should be making. Honestly, that’s the trickiest part of motivation reads. Just try to be aware of what assumptions you are making. What would have to be true for this read to be true? Or more specifically, what would invalidate this read? What are its weaknesses? How can it be wrong? That way, if you’re aware of how your reads can go sideways and what specifically they are based on, you’ll immediately recognize it when you see something that tells you your reads might be wrong and you have to reconsider. 
[quote name=Socoobo url=https://aops.com/community/p17034160]
These subtle things make me think NDMath is definitely not w/w with Fvr_Vain.[/quote]
I would honestly hesitate before you use subtle tells in “definite” reads. I don’t know whether these were your actual reads, or whether you privately were more reserved, but it’s something to think about. Granted, it was LYLO where you don’t have much other choice, but still. 

All of this is concerning motivation reads, which are honestly really hard to get right. There’s not really much else I can say about them, except that better reads will come with experience. You did decently well this game, and you put in decent effort. You also seem to have a good grasp on how the game works, general gameplay, your wincons, and most common mistakes, although voting early in LYLO was unfortunate. Still, good job. It was a nice try. Better luck in your future games, and I hope this helped!
